As merged, real bandits spawn, camp, and stay stuck. Attack resolution / loot split / blueprint reward / target death cleanup are all DEAD CODE in the live path. |
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7325:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6081:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10907:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7326:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6082:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10939:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7381:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6386:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3511:+        emit BlueprintEarned(clanId, banditId, world.blueprintUnit(), world.getWorldState().currentTick);
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7392:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6397:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3681:+            "one blueprint per defeated bandit"
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7408:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6505:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7812:packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:290:        emit BlueprintEarned(clanId, banditId, world.blueprintUnit(), world.getWorldState().currentTick);
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7409:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6516:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8291:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7410:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6520:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8327:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7411:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6524:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:6:**NEEDS_FIXES.** The phase ships a mostly-coherent bandit subsystem, but it has a critical architectural gap: bandits **never actually attack in production**. There is code for spawn, camp transition, attack resolution, defender split, blueprint reward, and target-death cleanup — but nothing in `heartbeat()` ever transitions a Camped bandit into the Attacking state, so all of `_resolveAttackingBandits` / `_resolveBanditAttack` / loot / blueprint / cleanup logic is unreachable from a normal heartbeat. The defender registry, simulation parity, seed timing, and arithmetic all look sound otherwise. Recommend a single follow-up fix to wire Camped→Attacking dispatch (or explicit acknowledgment that Wave-N attack dispatch is deferred to a sub-issue) before merging.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7413:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6533:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:61:1. **Phase delivers ~85% of the stated bandit goal.** State machine ✓, spawn ✓, eager-settle ✓, attack resolution mechanics ✓, defender split ✓, blueprint reward ✓, target-death cleanup ✓. Missing piece: target-selection + Camped→Attacking dispatch in production heartbeat (H1). All six newly added Solidity events for the attack lifecycle (`BanditTargetDied`, `WallDamagedByBandit`, `ClansmanKilledByBandit`, `BlueprintEarned`, `LootDistributed`) are unreachable until that gap closes — Convex indexer wiring for those events is therefore untested in integration.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7441:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6680:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3546:+        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).blueprintBalance, blueprintBefore + 2, "one blueprint per defeated bandit");
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7469:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6740:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5760:docs/reviews/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3546:+        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).blueprintBalance, blueprintBefore + 2, "one blueprint per defeated bandit");
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7496:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6785:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6481:docs/reviews/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3546:+        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).blueprintBalance, blueprintBefore + 2, "one blueprint per defeated bandit");
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7536:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7008:docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:218:- `elder world snapshot` — `getWorldSnapshot()` — world tick, season clock, winter, leaderboard, bandit id
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7541:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7068:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_4_ui_indexer_getters.md:84:- Drives: top HUD bar, season clock, winter indicator, leaderboard panel, bandit summary
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7660:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7245:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:11:The phase ships ~85% of the bandit subsystem (state machine, spawn, attack resolution, defender split, blueprint reward, target-death cleanup). All five reviewers independently flagged the SAME critical gap: **production bandits never attack**. They spawn → Camped → STUCK FOREVER. All the attack/loot/blueprint code is dead in the live heartbeat path; only test harnesses force the Camped→Attacking transition.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7661:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7246:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:21:| H1 | `ClanWorld.sol:1551, 1654, 1679` | 5.4 + 5.5 + 4.7 + Gemini = **4/5** | HIGH | **Camped→Attacking dispatch missing in production heartbeat.** `_transitionBanditToAttacking` only called from test harnesses. `_advanceBanditStates` only handles Spawned→Camped + Resting→Camped. As merged, real bandits spawn, camp, and stay stuck. Attack resolution / loot split / blueprint reward / target death cleanup are all DEAD CODE in the live path. |
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7662:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7247:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:24:| H4 | `ClanWorld.sol:1755` + `1005-1016` | 5.4 = **1/5** | HIGH | **Blueprint reward unit mismatch.** Bandit defeat awards `targetClan.blueprintBalance += 1`. Monument upgrades consume `1e18` blueprint units. So one bandit win = 1 wei of blueprint. The new test at `BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:252-261` LOCKS THE BUG IN. Fix: award `1e18` and update test/event expectations. |
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7663:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7251:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:52:1. **The Phase delivers ~85% of stated bandit goal.** State machine, spawn, eager-settle, attack resolution mechanics, defender split, blueprint reward, target-death cleanup — all present and individually tested. The missing 15% is the production wiring of Camped→Attacking dispatch + escape→rest recovery. This is the H1 finding.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7696:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7420:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_numbered_implementation_plan.md:363:**Cut line:** blueprint gating can be deferred temporarily if bandit loop is not ready yet.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7834:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8045:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3066:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8650:The direct fix targets look clean: `_applyUpkeep()` and `_simulateApplyUpkeep()` use `_isWinterTick(tick)`, blueprint reward uses `BLUEPRINT_UNIT`, and `_resolveBanditAttack()` re-checks bandit state/target after settlement.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7849:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8269:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3966:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7812:packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:290:        emit BlueprintEarned(clanId, banditId, world.blueprintUnit(), world.getWorldState().currentTick);
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7850:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8395:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4493:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14959:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1755` and `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1005-1016` — Blueprint rewards use the wrong unit. Bandit defeat credits `targetClan.blueprintBalance += 1`, but monument upgrades consume `1e18` blueprint units. That makes one bandit win worth one wei of blueprint, and the new test in `packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:252-261` locks that bug in. Suggested fix: award `1e18` per blueprint reward and update the event/test expectations to the same unit.

That makes one bandit win worth one wei of blueprint, and the new test in `packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:252-261` locks that bug in. Suggested fix: award `1e18` per blueprint reward and update the event/test expectations to the same unit.

All five reviewers independently flagged the SAME critical gap: **production bandits never attack**. They spawn → Camped → STUCK FOREVER. All the attack/loot/blueprint code is dead in the live heartbeat path; only test harnesses force the Camped→Attacking transition.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10746:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7325:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6081:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10907:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10747:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7326:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6082:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10939:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10749:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7411:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6524:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:6:**NEEDS_FIXES.** The phase ships a mostly-coherent bandit subsystem, but it has a critical architectural gap: bandits **never actually attack in production**. There is code for spawn, camp transition, attack resolution, defender split, blueprint reward, and target-death cleanup — but nothing in `heartbeat()` ever transitions a Camped bandit into the Attacking state, so all of `_resolveAttackingBandits` / `_resolveBanditAttack` / loot / blueprint / cleanup logic is unreachable from a normal heartbeat. The defender registry, simulation parity, seed timing, and arithmetic all look sound otherwise. Recommend a single follow-up fix to wire Camped→Attacking dispatch (or explicit acknowledgment that Wave-N attack dispatch is deferred to a sub-issue) before merging.

All five reviewers independently flagged the SAME critical gap: **production bandits never attack**. They spawn → Camped → STUCK FOREVER. All the attack/loot/blueprint code is dead in the live heartbeat path; only test harnesses force the Camped→Attacking transition.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10829:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6524:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:6:**NEEDS_FIXES.** The phase ships a mostly-coherent bandit subsystem, but it has a critical architectural gap: bandits **never actually attack in production**. There is code for spawn, camp transition, attack resolution, defender split, blueprint reward, and target-death cleanup — but nothing in `heartbeat()` ever transitions a Camped bandit into the Attacking state, so all of `_resolveAttackingBandits` / `_resolveBanditAttack` / loot / blueprint / cleanup logic is unreachable from a normal heartbeat. The defender registry, simulation parity, seed timing, and arithmetic all look sound otherwise. Recommend a single follow-up fix to wire Camped→Attacking dispatch (or explicit acknowledgment that Wave-N attack dispatch is deferred to a sub-issue) before merging.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10830:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6534:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:464:VERDICT: **NEEDS_FIXES**. While the Phase 9 PR successfully integrates foundational spawn chance logic and deterministic attack resolution, it suffers from critical architectural drift regarding the bandit state machine and eager-settlement scope. Bandits lack automated transitions to select targets or recover from escapes, rendering the threat entirely inert in practice. Additionally, wall defense calculations and view-only settlement simulations contain edge cases that undermine base progression and UI accuracy.

docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8631:NEEDS_FIXES. The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8638:1. `ClanWorld.sol:2156`, `ClanWorld.sol:2169`, `ClanWorld.sol:1708` — Camped bandits still choose attack targets from stale raw clan state.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8639:`_eagerSettleForBandits()` only settles spawn-candidate regions, gated by spawn cooldown and RNG. A camped bandit in a non-candidate/cooldowned region then calls `_pickBanditAttackTarget()` over raw `_clans` and `_lootValueRaw()`. If a stale rich clan should already be dead or depleted, it can soak target selection; after `_settleClan(targetClanId)` the bandit escapes instead of attacking another live target in the same region. Fix by settling camped-bandit regions before target selection, or by target-picking from simulated settled clan state.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8650:The direct fix targets look clean: `_applyUpkeep()` and `_simulateApplyUpkeep()` use `_isWinterTick(tick)`, blueprint reward uses `BLUEPRINT_UNIT`, and `_resolveBanditAttack()` re-checks bandit state/target after settlement.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8662:NEEDS_FIXES. The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8669:1. `ClanWorld.sol:2156`, `ClanWorld.sol:2169`, `ClanWorld.sol:1708` — Camped bandits still choose attack targets from stale raw clan state.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8670:`_eagerSettleForBandits()` only settles spawn-candidate regions, gated by spawn cooldown and RNG. A camped bandit in a non-candidate/cooldowned region then calls `_pickBanditAttackTarget()` over raw `_clans` and `_lootValueRaw()`. If a stale rich clan should already be dead or depleted, it can soak target selection; after `_settleClan(targetClanId)` the bandit escapes instead of attacking another live target in the same region. Fix by settling camped-bandit regions before target selection, or by target-picking from simulated settled clan state.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8681:The direct fix targets look clean: `_applyUpkeep()` and `_simulateApplyUpkeep()` use `_isWinterTick(tick)`, blueprint reward uses `BLUEPRINT_UNIT`, and `_resolveBanditAttack()` re-checks bandit state/target after settlement.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:23:- `REGION_UNICORN_TOWN` — excluded from bandit spawn
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:24:- `REGION_DEEP_SEA` — excluded from bandit spawn
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:32:### test_banditSpawn_rejectsUnicornTownAndDeepSea
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:36:**Action:** Advance tick (heartbeat). Observe region assigned to spawned bandit (or absence of spawn).
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:38:**Assert:** Either (a) no bandit spawns (spawn skipped due to excluded region) OR (b) bandit's `currentRegion` is NOT Unicorn Town and NOT Deep Sea.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:46:### test_banditSpawn_maxOneActiveBanditAtOnce
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:48:**Setup:** Spawn one bandit (probability 100%, valid region). Confirm `activeBanditId != 0`.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:52:**Assert:** `WorldState.activeBanditId` unchanged (same bandit ID); no second bandit created. (v4.2 §15 invariant.)
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:56:### test_banditSpawn_setsStateAndNextActionTick
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:60:**Action:** Advance tick → bandit spawns.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:70:### test_banditCamping_doesNotAdvanceBeforeNextActionTick
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:72:**Setup:** Spawn bandit at tick N with `nextActionTick = N + 3`.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:80:### test_banditTransition_campingToAttacking_atNextActionTick
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:82:**Setup:** Spawn bandit at tick N, `nextActionTick = N + 2`. Mint a clan in the bandit's region (so target selection can succeed).
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:90:### test_banditSpawn_nextEligibleTickAdvances
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:92:**Setup:** After bandit spawns, read `WorldState.nextBanditSpawnEligibleTick`.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:100:### test_eagerSettle_banditTargetVaultReflectsSettledState
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:103:- Mint two clans, both in same region as incoming bandit.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:106:- Spawn bandit, advance to attack tick.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:112:- Specifically: if Clan A's vault would be higher post-settle and that increases its loot value, the bandit targeted it (or loot drawn is the correct settled amount).
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:125:**Assert:** At bandit target-pick step, Clan B's worker is included in defender registry with correct `defensePoints` (earned via prior settle).
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:183:**Setup:** Register worker W as defender of Clan A. Let bandit attack and resolve (defender wins or loses). Do not interrupt W.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:185:**Action:** Advance several ticks. Let second bandit spawn and attack same base.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:205:- Register N defenders at defender clan's base; their combined `defensePoints` > bandit's `attackPower`.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:206:- Spawn bandit targeting defender clan.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:221:- Spawn bandit.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:228:- Defender clan vault decremented by bandit carry amount.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:236:- Snapshot full state at start of tick T (bandit + defenders + seed).
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:247:**Setup:** Same bandit + defender state, but different `tickSeed` (advance one extra block to change block hash before deriving seed).
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:268:- Defenders win (combined 4 > bandit attack power).
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:289:**Setup:** Resolve one bandit attack. Read `defenseContributionsByBanditId[banditId]`.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:404:score = vaultGold + (workers * N) + (wallTier * M) + (monumentTier * K) - banditDamageTaken
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:450:1. Advance tick → bandit spawns in Clan B's region.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:451:2. Advance `CAMPING_DURATION` ticks → bandit transitions to Attacking.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:460:- `defenseContributionsByBanditId[banditId]` empty after resolution.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:461:- `activeBanditId == 0` (bandit cleared from world state on defeat/retreat).
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:467:**Fuzzer:** For any sequence of heartbeats, `WorldState.activeBanditId != 0` for at most one bandit ID at any point.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:473:### test_invariant_banditNeverInExcludedRegion
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:475:**Fuzzer:** For any spawned bandit, `BanditTroop.currentRegion != REGION_UNICORN_TOWN && != REGION_DEEP_SEA`.
docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:502:- The `_eagerSettleForBandit(banditId)` call MUST happen before target selection AND before damage application (3.E2). Tests 3.E2-a and 3.E2-b directly verify this ordering.

docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10168:   179	ERC20 balances are **not** the authoritative representation of a clan’s vault or a clansman’s wheelbarrow.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10266:The biggest thing I’m seeing is not reentrancy; the guards and self-call wrappers mostly do their job. The more serious fracture is accounting: market buys/sells are wired to clan vault balances while the canonical market semantics and the new carry-cap validation point at clansman carry, which creates odd gameplay and bandit-timing side effects.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10325:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1656`, `1771`, `1824`, `1946` — Market trades bypass clansman carry and mutate clan vaults directly. Spec says `MarketSell` source is worker carry and `MarketBuy` destination is worker carry (`docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:565-585`). Current code lets a worker in Unicorn Town sell homebase vault resources without carrying them, and bought resources appear in the vault without a return/deposit trip. This also changes bandit loot/target timing because vault value changes immediately.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10347:MEV/front-running is intentionally part of the design per §5.7/§11.2, but that makes the missing same-tick immediate-vs-scheduled collision test more important.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10360:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1656`, `1771`, `1824`, `1946` — Market trades bypass clansman carry and mutate clan vaults directly. Spec says `MarketSell` source is worker carry and `MarketBuy` destination is worker carry (`docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:565-585`). Current code lets a worker in Unicorn Town sell homebase vault resources without carrying them, and bought resources appear in the vault without a return/deposit trip. This also changes bandit loot/target timing because vault value changes immediately.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10382:MEV/front-running is intentionally part of the design per §5.7/§11.2, but that makes the missing same-tick immediate-vs-scheduled collision test more important.

docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:2796:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8631:NEEDS_FIXES. The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:2797:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8662:NEEDS_FIXES. The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.

docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3297:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8182:The source pass is down to two likely review items: one in view-simulation starvation handling, and one possible stale-state issue during target selection before a camped bandit attacks. I’m waiting on the parallel passes now to see whether they independently confirm those or uncover anything else in the ABI/tests lane.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3299:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8291:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3301:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8327:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3351:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4585:    46	///         Phase 2 (market execution) and Phase 3 (bandits, winter damage) are stubbed.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3455:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3477:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3853:///         Phase 2 is implemented; Phase 3 (bandits, winter damage) remains stubbed.

docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3548:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2992:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5423:/bin/bash -lc 'rg -n "test_e2e_banditLifecycle|test_winterStarvationReplay|test_resolveBanditAttackReturns|test_defeatedBanditAwardsBlueprint|test_deadTargetCleanup|test_heartbeat_scheduledMarketObserves" packages/contracts/test' in /home/claude/code/clan-world
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3552:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3685:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:22:`ClanWorld.sol:~525` overload is `function _markClanDead(uint32 clanId, string memory, uint64 tick, uint32 excludedBanditId)` — the named-by-position-only `string memory` argument is intentional (forge-lint workaround), but it means callers pass `"starvation"` / `"bandit"` / `"unknown"` and that information is dropped on the floor. The emitted `ClanEliminated(clanId, tick)` event has no cause field. If an operator wants to know whether a clan died from starvation vs. bandit, they must replay logs from preceding `BanditAttackResolved` / `ClansmanKilledByBandit` events. Either add a cause field to `ClanEliminated`, or remove the parameter entirely so the API doesn't pretend to track something it discards.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3553:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3692:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**

docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3866:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14952:NEEDS_FIXES. The biggest blocker is that the production heartbeat still does not drive the full bandit lifecycle: real bandits can spawn and camp, but there is no live path to target selection/attack, and escaped bandits never re-enter the cycle. There are also two deploy-safety issues outside the contract core: winter starvation replay is wrong for historically-settled ticks, and the repo still contains stale handwritten ABIs that will misdecode the new `WorldState` shape.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3868:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14994:NEEDS_FIXES. The biggest blocker is that the production heartbeat still does not drive the full bandit lifecycle: real bandits can spawn and camp, but there is no live path to target selection/attack, and escaped bandits never re-enter the cycle. There are also two deploy-safety issues outside the contract core: winter starvation replay is wrong for historically-settled ticks, and the repo still contains stale handwritten ABIs that will misdecode the new `WorldState` shape.

Either add cause field to event, or remove the parameter. |

The emitted `ClanEliminated(clanId, tick)` event has no cause field. If an operator wants to know whether a clan died from starvation vs. bandit, they must replay logs from preceding `BanditAttackResolved` / `ClansmanKilledByBandit` events. Either add a cause field to `ClanEliminated`, or remove the parameter entirely so the API doesn't pretend to track something it discards.

docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8296:    48	## A4. Eager-settle scope for bandit target selection
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8298:    50	Before bandit target selection is computed in a region, **all clans with homebases in the bandit's current region must be eagerly settled to that tick**.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8300:    52	This requirement exists because bandit targeting depends on each base's current vault loot-value, and accurate loot-value requires up-to-date clan settlement.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8351:   103	- players are expected to maintain a real vault buffer rather than rely on same-tick rescue deliveries
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8619:    48	///         Phase 2 is implemented; Phase 3 (bandits, winter damage) remains stubbed.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9338:  2072	            banditId: 0,
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:9586:  2320	            banditId: 0,
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10017:     8	    uint256 internal constant DOMAIN_BANDIT_SPAWN = uint256(keccak256("clanworld.bandit.spawn.v1"));
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-opus-4-7.md:112:`_clearWallUpgradeReservation` only deletes the reservation struct and decrements the pending counter — it does NOT credit vault. Currently this resource-burn path is unreachable in normal play (validator's pending counter prevents reserving past max with ≤ 4 clansmen per clan), but **Phase 9 attack-resolution will introduce wall-destruction writes**, creating a real race: queue UpgradeWall at L4 → bandit destroys wall to L3 between queue and settle → wall is back at L4 (then settle overwrites with `old+1=4` which is fine actually).

docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2033:packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:47:///         Phase 2 is implemented; Phase 3 (bandits, winter damage) remains stubbed.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2149:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5368:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8631:NEEDS_FIXES. The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.

docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2159:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5627:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:2797:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8662:NEEDS_FIXES. The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2164:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5633:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3299:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8291:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2165:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5634:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3301:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8327:NEEDS_FIXES. docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3079:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:2675:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5423:/bin/bash -lc 'rg -n "test_e2e_banditLifecycle|test_winterStarvationReplay|test_resolveBanditAttackReturns|test_defeatedBanditAwardsBlueprint|test_deadTargetCleanup|test_heartbeat_scheduledMarketObserves" packages/contracts/test' in /home/claude/code/clan-world
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3181:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:2796:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8631:NEEDS_FIXES. The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3182:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:2797:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8662:NEEDS_FIXES. The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3498:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3226:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5202:Reviewing the fix-round diff against the surrounding contract and TS client code now. I’m going to split this into parallel passes: one on the Solidity bandit/heartbeat path, one on ABI/TypeScript decode safety, and one on the new tests and starvation replay logic so we can verify the five prior HIGHs and look for regressions.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3499:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3227:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5203:  → Inspect changed Solidity bandit/heartbeat/starvation code in context and identify behavioral regressions
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3553:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3297:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8182:The source pass is down to two likely review items: one in view-simulation starvation handling, and one possible stale-state issue during target selection before a camped bandit attacks. I’m waiting on the parallel passes now to see whether they independently confirm those or uncover anything else in the ABI/tests lane.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3554:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3299:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8291:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3556:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3301:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8327:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3592:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3351:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4585:    46	///         Phase 2 (market execution) and Phase 3 (bandits, winter damage) are stubbed.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3667:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3455:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**

docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3748:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3548:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2992:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5423:/bin/bash -lc 'rg -n "test_e2e_banditLifecycle|test_winterStarvationReplay|test_resolveBanditAttackReturns|test_defeatedBanditAwardsBlueprint|test_deadTargetCleanup|test_heartbeat_scheduledMarketObserves" packages/contracts/test' in /home/claude/code/clan-world
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3752:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3552:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3685:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:22:`ClanWorld.sol:~525` overload is `function _markClanDead(uint32 clanId, string memory, uint64 tick, uint32 excludedBanditId)` — the named-by-position-only `string memory` argument is intentional (forge-lint workaround), but it means callers pass `"starvation"` / `"bandit"` / `"unknown"` and that information is dropped on the floor. The emitted `ClanEliminated(clanId, tick)` event has no cause field. If an operator wants to know whether a clan died from starvation vs. bandit, they must replay logs from preceding `BanditAttackResolved` / `ClansmanKilledByBandit` events. Either add a cause field to `ClanEliminated`, or remove the parameter entirely so the API doesn't pretend to track something it discards.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3753:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3553:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3692:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**

docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3923:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3770:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7072:    48	///         Phase 2 is implemented; Phase 3 (bandits, winter damage) remains stubbed.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3996:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3866:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14952:NEEDS_FIXES. The biggest blocker is that the production heartbeat still does not drive the full bandit lifecycle: real bandits can spawn and camp, but there is no live path to target selection/attack, and escaped bandits never re-enter the cycle. There are also two deploy-safety issues outside the contract core: winter starvation replay is wrong for historically-settled ticks, and the repo still contains stale handwritten ABIs that will misdecode the new `WorldState` shape.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3998:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3868:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14994:NEEDS_FIXES. The biggest blocker is that the production heartbeat still does not drive the full bandit lifecycle: real bandits can spawn and camp, but there is no live path to target selection/attack, and escaped bandits never re-enter the cycle. There are also two deploy-safety issues outside the contract core: winter starvation replay is wrong for historically-settled ticks, and the repo still contains stale handwritten ABIs that will misdecode the new `WorldState` shape.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4002:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3872:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:34:| M2 | `ClanWorld.sol:~525` (`_markClanDead`) | 4.7 = **1/5** | MED | **`reason` parameter swallowed.** Caller passes "starvation"/"bandit"/"unknown" but `ClanEliminated` event has no cause field. Either add cause field to event, or remove the parameter. |
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4006:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3876:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:22:`ClanWorld.sol:~525` overload is `function _markClanDead(uint32 clanId, string memory, uint64 tick, uint32 excludedBanditId)` — the named-by-position-only `string memory` argument is intentional (forge-lint workaround), but it means callers pass `"starvation"` / `"bandit"` / `"unknown"` and that information is dropped on the floor. The emitted `ClanEliminated(clanId, tick)` event has no cause field. If an operator wants to know whether a clan died from starvation vs. bandit, they must replay logs from preceding `BanditAttackResolved` / `ClansmanKilledByBandit` events. Either add a cause field to `ClanEliminated`, or remove the parameter entirely so the API doesn't pretend to track something it discards.

docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5294:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8631:NEEDS_FIXES. The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5295:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8662:NEEDS_FIXES. The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5297:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:22:`ClanWorld.sol:~525` overload is `function _markClanDead(uint32 clanId, string memory, uint64 tick, uint32 excludedBanditId)` — the named-by-position-only `string memory` argument is intentional (forge-lint workaround), but it means callers pass `"starvation"` / `"bandit"` / `"unknown"` and that information is dropped on the floor. The emitted `ClanEliminated(clanId, tick)` event has no cause field. If an operator wants to know whether a clan died from starvation vs. bandit, they must replay logs from preceding `BanditAttackResolved` / `ClansmanKilledByBandit` events. Either add a cause field to `ClanEliminated`, or remove the parameter entirely so the API doesn't pretend to track something it discards.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5382:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5202:Reviewing the fix-round diff against the surrounding contract and TS client code now. I’m going to split this into parallel passes: one on the Solidity bandit/heartbeat path, one on ABI/TypeScript decode safety, and one on the new tests and starvation replay logic so we can verify the five prior HIGHs and look for regressions.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5383:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5203:  → Inspect changed Solidity bandit/heartbeat/starvation code in context and identify behavioral regressions
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5441:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8182:The source pass is down to two likely review items: one in view-simulation starvation handling, and one possible stale-state issue during target selection before a camped bandit attacks. I’m waiting on the parallel passes now to see whether they independently confirm those or uncover anything else in the ABI/tests lane.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5442:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8291:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5444:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8327:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.

docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5644:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:34:| M2 | `ClanWorld.sol:~525` (`_markClanDead`) | 4.7 = **1/5** | MED | **`reason` parameter swallowed.** Caller passes "starvation"/"bandit"/"unknown" but `ClanEliminated` event has no cause field. Either add cause field to event, or remove the parameter. |
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5648:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5689:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4585:    46	///         Phase 2 (market execution) and Phase 3 (bandits, winter damage) are stubbed.

There are also two deploy-safety issues outside the contract core: winter starvation replay is wrong for historically-settled ticks, and the repo still contains stale handwritten ABIs that will misdecode the new `WorldState` shape.

docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5958:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6202:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5925:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:5:**Scope:** World layout, tick semantics, settlement rules, missions, resources, market execution, bandits, winter, building, victory, human steering, trust model, spawn rules, elimination rules, pool seeding, and season lifecycle.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5959:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6516:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8291:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5960:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6520:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8327:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5966:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6919:docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_agent_runner_spec.md:430:- The game rewards monument-building, but you must survive winters and bandits to get there.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5967:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6929:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5969:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7008:docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:218:- `elder world snapshot` — `getWorldSnapshot()` — world tick, season clock, winter, leaderboard, bandit id
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5970:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7068:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_4_ui_indexer_getters.md:84:- Drives: top HUD bar, season clock, winter indicator, leaderboard panel, bandit summary

Either add cause field to event, or remove the parameter. |

docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7167:3866:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14952:NEEDS_FIXES. The biggest blocker is that the production heartbeat still does not drive the full bandit lifecycle: real bandits can spawn and camp, but there is no live path to target selection/attack, and escaped bandits never re-enter the cycle. There are also two deploy-safety issues outside the contract core: winter starvation replay is wrong for historically-settled ticks, and the repo still contains stale handwritten ABIs that will misdecode the new `WorldState` shape.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7168:3868:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14994:NEEDS_FIXES. docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7219:3667:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3455:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**

The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7230:3996:docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3866:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14952:NEEDS_FIXES. The biggest blocker is that the production heartbeat still does not drive the full bandit lifecycle: real bandits can spawn and camp, but there is no live path to target selection/attack, and escaped bandits never re-enter the cycle. There are also two deploy-safety issues outside the contract core: winter starvation replay is wrong for historically-settled ticks, and the repo still contains stale handwritten ABIs that will misdecode the new `WorldState` shape.

docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7606:            banditId: 0,
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7697:`ClanState.DEAD` is the right anchor for Phase 7.5 OTC restrictions, and status codes were appended rather than inserted, which preserves existing numeric values. The death helper is close, but before merge it needs to become the single reliable lifecycle path for starvation/cold/bandit death, including vault burn, mission cleanup, defender release, and consistent events.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7736:`ClanState.DEAD` is the right anchor for Phase 7.5 OTC restrictions, and status codes were appended rather than inserted, which preserves existing numeric values. The death helper is close, but before merge it needs to become the single reliable lifecycle path for starvation/cold/bandit death, including vault burn, mission cleanup, defender release, and consistent events.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:46:### M3 — `test_scheduledMarketFailure_doesNotAffectAnotherClan` does not actually prove same-tick isolation
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:48:In `ClanWorld.t.sol`, the test computes `tick1 = world.getActiveMission(csId1).settlesAtTick` and `tick2 = world.getActiveMission(csId2).settlesAtTick` independently and walks `while (currentTick <= maxTick)`. There is no `assertEq(tick1, tick2, ...)`. If the two clans happen to have different baseRegion travel distances to UT, the failing buy and the OK sell land on different ticks, and the test passes by trivially proving cross-tick independence — not the same-tick FIFO-poisoning property the name implies. Add `assertEq(tick1, tick2)` (force the configuration if needed) and assert that both events fire in the same heartbeat.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:50:### M4 — No test for bandit-killed clansman with a queued scheduled market action
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:52:Phase 9 ships bandits that can kill clansmen between mission submission and settlement. `_executeScheduledMarketActions` correctly checks `cs.state == ClansmanState.DEAD` (line 1391) and emits `ERR_INVALID_CLANSMAN`, but no test exercises the seam. With the bandit suite landed in PR #266, this integration point should have at least one regression test — kill the clansman after enqueue, run heartbeat, assert the queue entry no-ops and surrounding entries still execute.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:79:Per spec §8.3 ("no minOut in v1") this is **spec-compliant**. Worth flagging because it's an asymmetry with BUY's `maxGoldIn` and a soft attack surface for adversarial elders that try to sandwich same-tick scheduled sells with their own immediate trades (spec §11.2 explicitly admits immediate-may-front-run-scheduled). Document that this is intentional v1 behavior in the elder toolbelt docs so agents don't expect price guarantees on sells.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-synthesis.md:11:The market plumbing is structurally sound (immediate + scheduled FIFO, k-invariant AMM, heartbeat ordering preserved, 116/116 tests). All 3 reviewers independently flag the same fundamental finding: **market trades operate on the clan vault, not worker carry — violating spec §8.3-8.4.** The impl + tests are mutually consistent but the spec contract is broken. Implications cascade into bandit loot timing + physical-presence game design.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-synthesis.md:16:- Path A (spec-correct): rewrite trades to operate on worker carry. Invasive — touches 4 functions + ~15 tests + bandit interaction. ~1-2 hour fix-round.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-synthesis.md:55:4. Verify bandit-loot timing tests still pass (vault changes vs carry changes)
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-synthesis.md:61:2. Document tradeoff: simpler impl, instant bandit-loot-target updates, but loses physical-presence enforcement
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:28:5. Integration risks — interaction with Phase 5 vault state + Phase 9 bandit attack timing

docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7385:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:21:| H1 | `ClanWorld.sol:1551, 1654, 1679` | 5.4 + 5.5 + 4.7 + Gemini = **4/5** | HIGH | **Camped→Attacking dispatch missing in production heartbeat.** `_transitionBanditToAttacking` only called from test harnesses. `_advanceBanditStates` only handles Spawned→Camped + Resting→Camped. As merged, real bandits spawn, camp, and stay stuck. Attack resolution / loot split / blueprint reward / target death cleanup are all DEAD CODE in the live path. |
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7387:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:25:| H5 | `ClanWorld.sol:1717` | 5.5 = **1/5** | HIGH | **`_resolveBanditAttack` reverts on target-death-during-attack.** `_settleClan(targetClanId)` may kill the target → `_markClanDead` aborts the attacking bandit → state machine then tries to transition already-`Escaped` bandit again at `:1758`, which is invalid. Race window. Fix: re-check `targetClan.clanState` and `bandit.state/targetClanId` after target/defender settlement; return if cleanup already handled. |
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7390:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:19:`ClanWorld.sol:~2210-2230` documents the heartbeat as 5 steps ("1. Settle… 2. Execute markets… 3. Eager-settle… 4. Advance bandit timers and resolve closed-tick bandit/world events… 5. Increment tick and publish the next tick seed atomically"), but the body has 8 inline-numbered steps (`Step 1`…`Step 8`), bandit spawn evaluation is its own step, and `_resolveWorldEvents` runs at step 7 not step 4. Stale natspec → reviewer/operator confusion. Trivial fix.

docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10660:	1.	I made BanditState.NONE = 0 so bandit.state == NONE is the natural “no active bandit” check. v4.2 omitted this; adding it avoids the ambiguous default value.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10903:NEEDS_FIXES. The heartbeat/FIFO plumbing is close, but two core Phase 6 invariants are off: market trades are wired to clan vault balances instead of worker carry, and the new ERC20 boundary layer is only seeded, not actually bridged during swaps. There’s also spec drift in when scheduled actions enter the queue and how immediate actions surface mission state, which will leak into UI/indexer behavior and later bandit/upkeep interactions.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10907:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10924:- The settle-then-execute heartbeat ordering looks sound, and sorting by `commitSequence` should preserve deterministic same-tick FIFO execution.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10925:- The biggest integrated consequence is cross-phase: routing market buys/sells through vault balances instead of worker carry materially changes Phase 5 deposit logistics and Phase 9 bandit timing.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10935:NEEDS_FIXES. The heartbeat/FIFO plumbing is close, but two core Phase 6 invariants are off: market trades are wired to clan vault balances instead of worker carry, and the new ERC20 boundary layer is only seeded, not actually bridged during swaps. There’s also spec drift in when scheduled actions enter the queue and how immediate actions surface mission state, which will leak into UI/indexer behavior and later bandit/upkeep interactions.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10939:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10956:- The settle-then-execute heartbeat ordering looks sound, and sorting by `commitSequence` should preserve deterministic same-tick FIFO execution.
docs/reviews/r2-phase6/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10957:- The biggest integrated consequence is cross-phase: routing market buys/sells through vault balances instead of worker carry materially changes Phase 5 deposit logistics and Phase 9 bandit timing.

docs/planning/battleplan-v1.txt:14:	•	Detail view — chibi-style 24×24 clansman sprites with clan-color caps and tiny tools (axe, pickaxe, fishing rod, shield). Subtle environmental motion: smoke from chimneys, wheat swaying, waves at the shoreline, a windmill turning. Trees that visibly shake when chopped. Wheat plots that visibly empty as harvested. A bandit camp with a flickering campfire and an ominous flag.
docs/planning/battleplan-v1.txt:16:	•	Bandit drama — when bandits are projected to attack a base, that base pulses red, the bandit sprite gains a war-banner, and a small skull icon appears over the projected target. Failed defense = brief screen-shake + flash. Successful defense = bandit sprite explodes into a cloud of pixel-coins.
docs/planning/battleplan-v1.txt:23:	3.	Hard-code one WorldSnapshot with 4 clans, 12 clansmen, 3 missions in flight (one traveling, one gathering, one waiting), plus a camped bandit. ~30 min.

docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5202:Reviewing the fix-round diff against the surrounding contract and TS client code now. I’m going to split this into parallel passes: one on the Solidity bandit/heartbeat path, one on ABI/TypeScript decode safety, and one on the new tests and starvation replay logic so we can verify the five prior HIGHs and look for regressions.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5203:  → Inspect changed Solidity bandit/heartbeat/starvation code in context and identify behavioral regressions
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5225:packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:603:    event BlueprintAwarded(uint32 indexed clanId, uint32 indexed banditId, uint256 amount);

docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8182:The source pass is down to two likely review items: one in view-simulation starvation handling, and one possible stale-state issue during target selection before a camped bandit attacks. I’m waiting on the parallel passes now to see whether they independently confirm those or uncover anything else in the ABI/tests lane.

docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8291:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8295:  Heartbeat now correctly drives `Camped -> Attacking -> resolution`, but `_pickBanditAttackTarget()` still ranks candidates from raw `_clans` storage without first settling all competing clans to `closedTick`. The new eager-settle step only settles spawn-candidate regions and is skipped entirely at the global bandit cap, so live attacks can choose the wrong target based on stale loot/liveness, then resolve that wrong attack normally. The new end-to-end heartbeat test proves the lifecycle is live, but it only uses a single clan, so this competitive-selection bug is still untested.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8327:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8331:  Heartbeat now correctly drives `Camped -> Attacking -> resolution`, but `_pickBanditAttackTarget()` still ranks candidates from raw `_clans` storage without first settling all competing clans to `closedTick`. The new eager-settle step only settles spawn-candidate regions and is skipped entirely at the global bandit cap, so live attacks can choose the wrong target based on stale loot/liveness, then resolve that wrong attack normally. The new end-to-end heartbeat test proves the lifecycle is live, but it only uses a single clan, so this competitive-selection bug is still untested.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-synthesis.md:4:**Phase:** dev-phase-9-bandits — Bandit System
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-synthesis.md:18:**Single-model new HIGH (Codex 5.4 only):** `_pickBanditAttackTarget` ranks competing targets from RAW unsettled clan state. Eager-settle only covers spawn-candidate regions, skipped at global bandit cap. Multi-clan target selection can pick the wrong clan based on stale loot/liveness in production.
docs/reviews/pr194-r2-synthesis.md:30:| `ClanWorld.sol:1703, 2156, 2305` | 5.4 = 1/3 | HIGH (single-model) | **Stale-state competitive target selection.** `_pickBanditAttackTarget` reads raw `_clans` storage without settling competing clans first. With multiple clans in a region + bandit at cap, target choice diverges from actual loot. **Action:** file as follow-up issue, address before Submission 2 demo. |
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:23:Phase 9 release branch. Bundles all phase-9 sub-issues (bandit threat).
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:37:- Phase 9 intentionally redesigns the v1-prep bandit ABI: `BanditState` enum order/insertion and `BanditTroop` layout changed. ABI consumers must regenerate from the updated contract ABI.

docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4101:+        assertEq(uint8(bandit.state), uint8(BanditState.Spawned), "bandit spawned");
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4102:+        assertEq(bandit.region, ClanWorldConstants.REGION_FOREST, "forest selected after eager settle");
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4103:+        assertEq(bandit.tickEnteredState, closedTick, "spawn used closed tick");
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4130:+        uint256 forestRoll = world.banditSpawnRoll(seed, ClanWorldConstants.REGION_FOREST);
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4131:+        uint256 mountainRoll = world.banditSpawnRoll(seed, ClanWorldConstants.REGION_MOUNTAINS);
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4143:+        BanditTroop memory bandit = world.getBandit(1);
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4145:+        assertEq(uint8(bandit.state), uint8(BanditState.Spawned), "spawned state");
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4146:+        assertEq(bandit.tickEnteredState, state.currentTick, "entered current tick");
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4147:+        assertEq(state.activeBanditId, bandit.id, "active bandit set");
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4261:+        // Advance until the clan is truly >200 ticks behind. Phase 9 bandit
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4287:+        BanditTroop memory bandit = stub.getBandit(999);
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4289:+        assertEq(bandit.id, 0, "missing id");
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4290:+        assertEq(uint8(bandit.state), uint8(BanditState.None), "missing state");
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4465:I’ll use the GitHub review workflow shape here, plus parallel repo reads and subagents, since this is explicitly a final integrated review. I’m going to trace heartbeat, bandit state transitions, spawn targeting, attack resolution, and ABI/test seams against the actual files so the findings land with real line references.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3063:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8639:`_eagerSettleForBandits()` only settles spawn-candidate regions, gated by spawn cooldown and RNG. A camped bandit in a non-candidate/cooldowned region then calls `_pickBanditAttackTarget()` over raw `_clans` and `_lootValueRaw()`. If a stale rich clan should already be dead or depleted, it can soak target selection; after `_settleClan(targetClanId)` the bandit escapes instead of attacking another live target in the same region. Fix by settling camped-bandit regions before target selection, or by target-picking from simulated settled clan state.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3066:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8650:The direct fix targets look clean: `_applyUpkeep()` and `_simulateApplyUpkeep()` use `_isWinterTick(tick)`, blueprint reward uses `BLUEPRINT_UNIT`, and `_resolveBanditAttack()` re-checks bandit state/target after settlement.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3067:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8670:`_eagerSettleForBandits()` only settles spawn-candidate regions, gated by spawn cooldown and RNG. A camped bandit in a non-candidate/cooldowned region then calls `_pickBanditAttackTarget()` over raw `_clans` and `_lootValueRaw()`. If a stale rich clan should already be dead or depleted, it can soak target selection; after `_settleClan(targetClanId)` the bandit escapes instead of attacking another live target in the same region. Fix by settling camped-bandit regions before target selection, or by target-picking from simulated settled clan state.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3070:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8681:The direct fix targets look clean: `_applyUpkeep()` and `_simulateApplyUpkeep()` use `_isWinterTick(tick)`, blueprint reward uses `BLUEPRINT_UNIT`, and `_resolveBanditAttack()` re-checks bandit state/target after settlement.
