docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3591:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10583:23f7f1a:packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:24:        _bandits[id].targetClanId = targetClanId;
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3593:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10595:NEEDS_FIXES. The integrated phase has real blockers: production bandits never reach `Attacking`, TypeScript heartbeat/snapshot clients have stale tuple ABIs, and attack resolution can revert when target settlement kills the target mid-resolution. I would not merge until these are fixed and covered by production-path heartbeat tests.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3594:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10599:`packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1654` — Camped bandits never select a target or transition to `Attacking` in production. `_transitionBanditToAttacking` exists at `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1551`, but only harness tests call it; heartbeat `_advanceBanditStates` only handles `Spawned -> Camped` and `Resting -> Camped`, so `_resolveAttackingBandits` at `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1679` never runs for real spawned bandits. Add deterministic target selection for eligible camped bandits, call `_transitionBanditToAttacking`, and add an end-to-end spawn-to-attack heartbeat test without harness state mutation.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3595:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10605:`packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1717` — `_resolveBanditAttack` can revert if `_settleClan(targetClanId)` kills the target. Target death calls `_markClanDead`, which aborts attacking bandits for that target at `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:576`; then `_resolveBanditAttack` continues and may try to transition the already-`Escaped` bandit again at `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1758`, which is invalid. Re-check `targetClan.clanState` and `bandit.state/targetClanId` after target and defender settlement, returning if cleanup already handled the bandit.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3596:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10637:NEEDS_FIXES. The integrated phase has real blockers: production bandits never reach `Attacking`, TypeScript heartbeat/snapshot clients have stale tuple ABIs, and attack resolution can revert when target settlement kills the target mid-resolution. I would not merge until these are fixed and covered by production-path heartbeat tests.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3597:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10641:`packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1654` — Camped bandits never select a target or transition to `Attacking` in production. `_transitionBanditToAttacking` exists at `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1551`, but only harness tests call it; heartbeat `_advanceBanditStates` only handles `Spawned -> Camped` and `Resting -> Camped`, so `_resolveAttackingBandits` at `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1679` never runs for real spawned bandits. Add deterministic target selection for eligible camped bandits, call `_transitionBanditToAttacking`, and add an end-to-end spawn-to-attack heartbeat test without harness state mutation.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3598:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10647:`packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1717` — `_resolveBanditAttack` can revert if `_settleClan(targetClanId)` kills the target. Target death calls `_markClanDead`, which aborts attacking bandits for that target at `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:576`; then `_resolveBanditAttack` continues and may try to transition the already-`Escaped` bandit again at `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1758`, which is invalid. Re-check `targetClan.clanState` and `bandit.state/targetClanId` after target and defender settlement, returning if cleanup already handled the bandit.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3646:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7385:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:21:| H1 | `ClanWorld.sol:1551, 1654, 1679` | 5.4 + 5.5 + 4.7 + Gemini = **4/5** | HIGH | **Camped→Attacking dispatch missing in production heartbeat.** `_transitionBanditToAttacking` only called from test harnesses. `_advanceBanditStates` only handles Spawned→Camped + Resting→Camped. As merged, real bandits spawn, camp, and stay stuck. Attack resolution / loot split / blueprint reward / target death cleanup are all DEAD CODE in the live path. |
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3647:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7387:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:25:| H5 | `ClanWorld.sol:1717` | 5.5 = **1/5** | HIGH | **`_resolveBanditAttack` reverts on target-death-during-attack.** `_settleClan(targetClanId)` may kill the target → `_markClanDead` aborts the attacking bandit → state machine then tries to transition already-`Escaped` bandit again at `:1758`, which is invalid. Race window. Fix: re-check `targetClan.clanState` and `bandit.state/targetClanId` after target/defender settlement; return if cleanup already handled. |
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3680:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:6:**NEEDS_FIXES.** The phase ships a mostly-coherent bandit subsystem, but it has a critical architectural gap: bandits **never actually attack in production**. There is code for spawn, camp transition, attack resolution, defender split, blueprint reward, and target-death cleanup — but nothing in `heartbeat()` ever transitions a Camped bandit into the Attacking state, so all of `_resolveAttackingBandits` / `_resolveBanditAttack` / loot / blueprint / cleanup logic is unreachable from a normal heartbeat. The defender registry, simulation parity, seed timing, and arithmetic all look sound otherwise. Recommend a single follow-up fix to wire Camped→Attacking dispatch (or explicit acknowledgment that Wave-N attack dispatch is deferred to a sub-issue) before merging.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3682:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:11:`packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1551` (`_transitionBanditToAttacking`) is **only called from test harnesses** (`Bandit.t.sol:18`, `BanditAttackResolution.t.sol`, `BanditSpawn.t.sol`). The heartbeat steps 4–6 (`_advanceBanditStates`, `_resolveAttackingBandits`, `_evaluateBanditSpawns`, `ClanWorld.sol:2243-2252`) only handle Spawned→Camped, Resting→Camped, attack-resolution-of-already-Attacking, and new spawns. There is no target-selection + state-transition step for Camped bandits, so on the live engine bandits spawn, transition to Camped, and remain there forever. Consequently `_resolveAttackingBandits`, `_resolveBanditAttack`, `_distributeBanditLootToDefendingClans`, `BlueprintEarned`, `_releaseDefendersForDeadTarget`, `_abortBanditAttacksForDeadTarget`, and the `BanditTargetDied` event are **dead code in the live heartbeat path**. The PR description claims "Pending sub-issues: 9.4 Deterministic attack resolution," but the merged commit `d02061f feat(contracts): deterministic bandit attack resolution (#207)` is part of this PR — so the gap is dispatch wiring, not resolution logic. **Suggested fix:** add a step between `_advanceBanditStates` and `_resolveAttackingBandits` that, for each Camped bandit whose camp window has elapsed, picks a target via `_banditSpawnRegionWeights`-style scoring (or the loot-value heuristic in `_lootValueRaw`) and calls `_transitionBanditToAttacking`. Alternatively, file a follow-up issue and add a top-of-file comment in the heartbeat docstring explicitly marking attack dispatch as deferred so reviewers and the Convex indexer don't assume the system is live.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3685:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:22:`ClanWorld.sol:~525` overload is `function _markClanDead(uint32 clanId, string memory, uint64 tick, uint32 excludedBanditId)` — the named-by-position-only `string memory` argument is intentional (forge-lint workaround), but it means callers pass `"starvation"` / `"bandit"` / `"unknown"` and that information is dropped on the floor. The emitted `ClanEliminated(clanId, tick)` event has no cause field. If an operator wants to know whether a clan died from starvation vs. bandit, they must replay logs from preceding `BanditAttackResolved` / `ClansmanKilledByBandit` events. Either add a cause field to `ClanEliminated`, or remove the parameter entirely so the API doesn't pretend to track something it discards.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3689:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:61:1. **Phase delivers ~85% of the stated bandit goal.** State machine ✓, spawn ✓, eager-settle ✓, attack resolution mechanics ✓, defender split ✓, blueprint reward ✓, target-death cleanup ✓. Missing piece: target-selection + Camped→Attacking dispatch in production heartbeat (H1). All six newly added Solidity events for the attack lifecycle (`BanditTargetDied`, `WallDamagedByBandit`, `ClansmanKilledByBandit`, `BlueprintEarned`, `LootDistributed`) are unreachable until that gap closes — Convex indexer wiring for those events is therefore untested in integration.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3692:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3698:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:116:2. **`_resolveAttackingBandits` array iteration is correct.** Uses `while (i < length)` with `i++` only when the bandit is NOT deleted (stays at same index if deleted, because swap-pop fills the slot). This is the correct pattern for iterate-and-delete on a dynamic array.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3699:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:118:3. **`_markClanDead` cascade design is sound.** The `excludedBanditId` parameter prevents `_abortBanditAttacksForDeadTarget` from aborting the bandit that *caused* the death (since it's already being resolved). This prevents re-entrancy into the bandit state machine during attack resolution.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3701:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:470:Although sub-issues 9.1 and 9.4 outline a complete state machine, the actual automated transitions driving it are missing. There is no logic in `_advanceBanditStates()` to transition a bandit from `Camped` to `Attacking` (Target Selection is completely unimplemented). Furthermore, when an attack resolves without a defeat and a bandit transitions to `Escaped`, there is no logic to transition it back to `Resting`. As a result, once spawned, a bandit will get permanently stuck in `Camped` (or, if forcefully triggered, stuck in `Escaped`).
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3702:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:471:*Suggested fix:* Implement target selection logic in `_advanceBanditStates()` to evaluate eligible `Camped` bandits, pick a valid target, and call `_transitionBanditToAttacking()`. Additionally, add a transition from `Escaped` to `Resting` either immediately during attack resolution or as an automated step in `_advanceBanditStates()`.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3703:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:482:The wall damage calculation restricts the maximum damage absorbed to exactly `WALL_HP_PER_LEVEL` (100) per attack, regardless of `clan.wallLevel`. Any incoming damage exceeding 100 instantly spills over to the base and clansmen, and the wall only loses exactly 1 level. This means a fully upgraded Level 5 wall offers no more immediate damage mitigation against a large bandit attack than a Level 1 wall, heavily undermining the defensive value of upgrading walls.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3705:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:487:The `_simulateSettleToTick` function simulates individual clans in isolation. If a clansman is on a `DefendBase` mission and the target clan is eliminated by bandits during the unsettled window, the real `_markClanDead` correctly releases those defenders. However, the view-only simulation has no visibility into the target clan's death and will incorrectly mock the clansman as `ACTING` (defending) indefinitely. This will cause `getClanFullView` and the UI to show clansmen stuck defending a dead base until their own clan is explicitly settled.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3708:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:494:The `BanditAttackResolved` event emits hardcoded `0`s for the `stolenWood`, `stolenIron`, `stolenWheat`, and `stolenFish` fields even if the bandit escapes successfully. If resource stealing is deferred to a future phase, it should be documented as a stub; otherwise, the bandit should actually drain resources from the target clan's vault upon an un-defeated attack.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3710:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:11:The phase ships ~85% of the bandit subsystem (state machine, spawn, attack resolution, defender split, blueprint reward, target-death cleanup). All five reviewers independently flagged the SAME critical gap: **production bandits never attack**. They spawn → Camped → STUCK FOREVER. All the attack/loot/blueprint code is dead in the live heartbeat path; only test harnesses force the Camped→Attacking transition.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3711:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:21:| H1 | `ClanWorld.sol:1551, 1654, 1679` | 5.4 + 5.5 + 4.7 + Gemini = **4/5** | HIGH | **Camped→Attacking dispatch missing in production heartbeat.** `_transitionBanditToAttacking` only called from test harnesses. `_advanceBanditStates` only handles Spawned→Camped + Resting→Camped. As merged, real bandits spawn, camp, and stay stuck. Attack resolution / loot split / blueprint reward / target death cleanup are all DEAD CODE in the live path. |
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3712:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:24:| H4 | `ClanWorld.sol:1755` + `1005-1016` | 5.4 = **1/5** | HIGH | **Blueprint reward unit mismatch.** Bandit defeat awards `targetClan.blueprintBalance += 1`. Monument upgrades consume `1e18` blueprint units. So one bandit win = 1 wei of blueprint. The new test at `BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:252-261` LOCKS THE BUG IN. Fix: award `1e18` and update test/event expectations. |
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3713:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:25:| H5 | `ClanWorld.sol:1717` | 5.5 = **1/5** | HIGH | **`_resolveBanditAttack` reverts on target-death-during-attack.** `_settleClan(targetClanId)` may kill the target → `_markClanDead` aborts the attacking bandit → state machine then tries to transition already-`Escaped` bandit again at `:1758`, which is invalid. Race window. Fix: re-check `targetClan.clanState` and `bandit.state/targetClanId` after target/defender settlement; return if cleanup already handled. |
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3716:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:52:1. **The Phase delivers ~85% of stated bandit goal.** State machine, spawn, eager-settle, attack resolution mechanics, defender split, blueprint reward, target-death cleanup — all present and individually tested. The missing 15% is the production wiring of Camped→Attacking dispatch + escape→rest recovery. This is the H1 finding.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3990:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8187:packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:306:            banditId = world.getWorldState().activeBanditId;
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3992:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8295:  Heartbeat now correctly drives `Camped -> Attacking -> resolution`, but `_pickBanditAttackTarget()` still ranks candidates from raw `_clans` storage without first settling all competing clans to `closedTick`. The new eager-settle step only settles spawn-candidate regions and is skipped entirely at the global bandit cap, so live attacks can choose the wrong target based on stale loot/liveness, then resolve that wrong attack normally. The new end-to-end heartbeat test proves the lifecycle is live, but it only uses a single clan, so this competitive-selection bug is still untested.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3998:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8331:  Heartbeat now correctly drives `Camped -> Attacking -> resolution`, but `_pickBanditAttackTarget()` still ranks candidates from raw `_clans` storage without first settling all competing clans to `closedTick`. The new eager-settle step only settles spawn-candidate regions and is skipped entirely at the global bandit cap, so live attacks can choose the wrong target based on stale loot/liveness, then resolve that wrong attack normally. The new end-to-end heartbeat test proves the lifecycle is live, but it only uses a single clan, so this competitive-selection bug is still untested.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4483:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14601:   778	If `totalDefense < banditAttack`:
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4492:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14955:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1551, 1654, 1679` — The state machine is incomplete in production. Heartbeat advances `Spawned -> Camped` and `Resting -> Camped`, but there is no non-test caller for `_transitionBanditToAttacking`, and no live caller ever moves `Escaped -> Resting`. The tests mask this by forcing state transitions through harness-only helpers in `packages/contracts/test/Bandit.t.sol` and `packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol`. As merged, real bandits never attack, and any escaped bandit would permanently occupy active/cap slots. Suggested fix: wire heartbeat-driven target selection/rampage transitions into the live contract and add at least one end-to-end test from natural spawn through attack and post-attack recovery.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4493:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14959:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1755` and `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1005-1016` — Blueprint rewards use the wrong unit. Bandit defeat credits `targetClan.blueprintBalance += 1`, but monument upgrades consume `1e18` blueprint units. That makes one bandit win worth one wei of blueprint, and the new test in `packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:252-261` locks that bug in. Suggested fix: award `1e18` per blueprint reward and update the event/test expectations to the same unit.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4496:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14997:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1551, 1654, 1679` — The state machine is incomplete in production. Heartbeat advances `Spawned -> Camped` and `Resting -> Camped`, but there is no non-test caller for `_transitionBanditToAttacking`, and no live caller ever moves `Escaped -> Resting`. The tests mask this by forcing state transitions through harness-only helpers in `packages/contracts/test/Bandit.t.sol` and `packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol`. As merged, real bandits never attack, and any escaped bandit would permanently occupy active/cap slots. Suggested fix: wire heartbeat-driven target selection/rampage transitions into the live contract and add at least one end-to-end test from natural spawn through attack and post-attack recovery.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4497:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:15001:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1755` and `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1005-1016` — Blueprint rewards use the wrong unit. Bandit defeat credits `targetClan.blueprintBalance += 1`, but monument upgrades consume `1e18` blueprint units. That makes one bandit win worth one wei of blueprint, and the new test in `packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:252-261` locks that bug in. Suggested fix: award `1e18` per blueprint reward and update the event/test expectations to the same unit.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5465:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:50:Before bandit target selection is computed in a region, **all clans with homebases in the bandit's current region must be eagerly settled to that tick**.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5466:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:52:This requirement exists because bandit targeting depends on each base's current vault loot-value, and accurate loot-value requires up-to-date clan settlement.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5648:docs/reviews/pr198-synthesis.md:11:The market plumbing is structurally sound (immediate + scheduled FIFO, k-invariant AMM, heartbeat ordering preserved, 116/116 tests). All 3 reviewers independently flag the same fundamental finding: **market trades operate on the clan vault, not worker carry — violating spec §8.3-8.4.** The impl + tests are mutually consistent but the spec contract is broken. Implications cascade into bandit loot timing + physical-presence game design.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5911:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8631:NEEDS_FIXES. The 5 prior HIGH fixes appear to have landed: heartbeat now drives bandit attacks, ABI tuple order is updated via generated ABI, winter starvation replay uses historical ticks, blueprint rewards are `1e18`, and the target-death attack race now returns safely. I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5913:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8650:The direct fix targets look clean: `_applyUpkeep()` and `_simulateApplyUpkeep()` use `_isWinterTick(tick)`, blueprint reward uses `BLUEPRINT_UNIT`, and `_resolveBanditAttack()` re-checks bandit state/target after settlement.

I found no new HIGH issues, but there are 2 MEDIUM lifecycle/data-consistency bugs and 1 LOW test gap.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6014:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7385:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:21:| H1 | `ClanWorld.sol:1551, 1654, 1679` | 5.4 + 5.5 + 4.7 + Gemini = **4/5** | HIGH | **Camped→Attacking dispatch missing in production heartbeat.** `_transitionBanditToAttacking` only called from test harnesses. `_advanceBanditStates` only handles Spawned→Camped + Resting→Camped. docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6081:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10907:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6082:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10939:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6250:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10325:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1656`, `1771`, `1824`, `1946` — Market trades bypass clansman carry and mutate clan vaults directly. Spec says `MarketSell` source is worker carry and `MarketBuy` destination is worker carry (`docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:565-585`). Current code lets a worker in Unicorn Town sell homebase vault resources without carrying them, and bought resources appear in the vault without a return/deposit trip. This also changes bandit loot/target timing because vault value changes immediately.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6252:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10360:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1656`, `1771`, `1824`, `1946` — Market trades bypass clansman carry and mutate clan vaults directly. Spec says `MarketSell` source is worker carry and `MarketBuy` destination is worker carry (`docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:565-585`). Current code lets a worker in Unicorn Town sell homebase vault resources without carrying them, and bought resources appear in the vault without a return/deposit trip. This also changes bandit loot/target timing because vault value changes immediately.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6258:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:48:In `ClanWorld.t.sol`, the test computes `tick1 = world.getActiveMission(csId1).settlesAtTick` and `tick2 = world.getActiveMission(csId2).settlesAtTick` independently and walks `while (currentTick <= maxTick)`. There is no `assertEq(tick1, tick2, ...)`. If the two clans happen to have different baseRegion travel distances to UT, the failing buy and the OK sell land on different ticks, and the test passes by trivially proving cross-tick independence — not the same-tick FIFO-poisoning property the name implies. Add `assertEq(tick1, tick2)` (force the configuration if needed) and assert that both events fire in the same heartbeat.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6259:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:79:Per spec §8.3 ("no minOut in v1") this is **spec-compliant**. Worth flagging because it's an asymmetry with BUY's `maxGoldIn` and a soft attack surface for adversarial elders that try to sandwich same-tick scheduled sells with their own immediate trades (spec §11.2 explicitly admits immediate-may-front-run-scheduled). Document that this is intentional v1 behavior in the elder toolbelt docs so agents don't expect price guarantees on sells.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6512:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8155:  2321	        // Step 3: Eager-settle bases and active defenders in bandit spawn-candidate regions.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6516:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8291:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6517:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8295:  Heartbeat now correctly drives `Camped -> Attacking -> resolution`, but `_pickBanditAttackTarget()` still ranks candidates from raw `_clans` storage without first settling all competing clans to `closedTick`. The new eager-settle step only settles spawn-candidate regions and is skipped entirely at the global bandit cap, so live attacks can choose the wrong target based on stale loot/liveness, then resolve that wrong attack normally. The new end-to-end heartbeat test proves the lifecycle is live, but it only uses a single clan, so this competitive-selection bug is still untested.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6520:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8327:NEEDS_FIXES. The five named HIGH fixes mostly landed: heartbeat now drives the bandit lifecycle, `WorldState`/`WorldSnapshot` tuple order is aligned in Solidity + generated ABI + TS consumers, winter starvation replay is using replayed ticks, blueprint reward is now `1e18`, and the target-death race no longer reverts. But there is still a new HIGH game-logic regression in production: camped bandits pick attack targets from stale raw clan state, so once the heartbeat path is live they can attack the wrong clan.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6521:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8331:  Heartbeat now correctly drives `Camped -> Attacking -> resolution`, but `_pickBanditAttackTarget()` still ranks candidates from raw `_clans` storage without first settling all competing clans to `closedTick`. The new eager-settle step only settles spawn-candidate regions and is skipped entirely at the global bandit cap, so live attacks can choose the wrong target based on stale loot/liveness, then resolve that wrong attack normally. The new end-to-end heartbeat test proves the lifecycle is live, but it only uses a single clan, so this competitive-selection bug is still untested.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6524:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:6:**NEEDS_FIXES.** The phase ships a mostly-coherent bandit subsystem, but it has a critical architectural gap: bandits **never actually attack in production**. There is code for spawn, camp transition, attack resolution, defender split, blueprint reward, and target-death cleanup — but nothing in `heartbeat()` ever transitions a Camped bandit into the Attacking state, so all of `_resolveAttackingBandits` / `_resolveBanditAttack` / loot / blueprint / cleanup logic is unreachable from a normal heartbeat. The defender registry, simulation parity, seed timing, and arithmetic all look sound otherwise. Recommend a single follow-up fix to wire Camped→Attacking dispatch (or explicit acknowledgment that Wave-N attack dispatch is deferred to a sub-issue) before merging.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6527:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:25:`ClanWorld.sol:~1813-1830`: the victim pool is the entire `_clanClansmanIds[clanId]` filtered only by `state != DEAD`. A clansman fishing in REGION_DEEP_SEA can die when a bandit attacks the home base. This may be intentional ("base raid causes generalized casualties"), but it's a notable design choice that the spec extract in `docs/reference/architecture-decisions.md` should confirm. If not intentional, restrict the pool to `cs.currentRegion == clan.baseRegion` (or `cs.state == ClansmanState.WAITING`/`DefendBase`).
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6533:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:61:1. **Phase delivers ~85% of the stated bandit goal.** State machine ✓, spawn ✓, eager-settle ✓, attack resolution mechanics ✓, defender split ✓, blueprint reward ✓, target-death cleanup ✓. Missing piece: target-selection + Camped→Attacking dispatch in production heartbeat (H1). All six newly added Solidity events for the attack lifecycle (`BanditTargetDied`, `WallDamagedByBandit`, `ClansmanKilledByBandit`, `BlueprintEarned`, `LootDistributed`) are unreachable until that gap closes — Convex indexer wiring for those events is therefore untested in integration.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6534:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:464:VERDICT: **NEEDS_FIXES**. While the Phase 9 PR successfully integrates foundational spawn chance logic and deterministic attack resolution, it suffers from critical architectural drift regarding the bandit state machine and eager-settlement scope. Bandits lack automated transitions to select targets or recover from escapes, rendering the threat entirely inert in practice. Additionally, wall defense calculations and view-only settlement simulations contain edge cases that undermine base progression and UI accuracy. 
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6535:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:475:Sub-issue 9.3 mandates eager-settling bases and defenders "before target selection" to ensure targets are chosen based on fresh, up-to-date loot values. However, `_eagerSettleForBandits()` includes an early return `if (_activeBanditCount >= MAX_TOTAL_BANDITS) return;`, completely bypassing all settlement when the global bandit cap is reached. Furthermore, it only settles regions that pass `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate()`—ignoring regions that have existing `Camped` bandits waiting to select targets but aren't currently eligible for a *new* spawn. This guarantees stale target evaluation.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6536:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:482:The wall damage calculation restricts the maximum damage absorbed to exactly `WALL_HP_PER_LEVEL` (100) per attack, regardless of `clan.wallLevel`. Any incoming damage exceeding 100 instantly spills over to the base and clansmen, and the wall only loses exactly 1 level. This means a fully upgraded Level 5 wall offers no more immediate damage mitigation against a large bandit attack than a Level 1 wall, heavily undermining the defensive value of upgrading walls.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6539:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:487:The `_simulateSettleToTick` function simulates individual clans in isolation. If a clansman is on a `DefendBase` mission and the target clan is eliminated by bandits during the unsettled window, the real `_markClanDead` correctly releases those defenders. However, the view-only simulation has no visibility into the target clan's death and will incorrectly mock the clansman as `ACTING` (defending) indefinitely. This will cause `getClanFullView` and the UI to show clansmen stuck defending a dead base until their own clan is explicitly settled.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6542:docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:31:2. **Drama over fidelity.** A cheap pulse-and-flash on a bandit-threatened base beats a perfectly-rendered combat animation. Make the moments unmissable.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6552:docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:195:| Resources become useful | World: wheelbarrow fill animation, deposit animation at base. Inspector: vault counter ticks up |
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6553:docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:197:| Bandits threaten | World: bandit camp sprite, red pulse on projected target base, attack countdown chip in HUD |

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6849:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7989:  2242	        // Step 3: Eager-settle bases and active defenders in bandit spawn-candidate regions.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6919:docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_agent_runner_spec.md:430:- The game rewards monument-building, but you must survive winters and bandits to get there.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6929:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6930:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:31:**Impact:** A bandit could spawn into a region where bases and defenders were NOT eager-settled, meaning the very next attack resolution sees stale clan state. This is a correctness bug, not a security bug — the clan will settle lazily later, but attack damage calculations against stale vaults/defender counts could be wrong.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6936:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:78:A wall with level 5 absorbs at most `WALL_HP_PER_LEVEL` (100) damage and drops exactly one level. This means a bandit with strength 500 against a level-5 wall will: absorb 100 via wall (drop to level 4), then pass 400 through to base defense and clansmen. The wall only provides a single level of protection per attack regardless of wall level. This may be intentional game design, but it means wall levels 2-5 only provide cosmetic insurance (multiple attacks needed to fully destroy the wall). Worth confirming this is the intended behavior since the wall cost scales significantly with level.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7008:docs/planning/V1/05 0G/clanworld_0g_battleplan_and_spec.md:218:- `elder world snapshot` — `getWorldSnapshot()` — world tick, season clock, winter, leaderboard, bandit id
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7068:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_4_ui_indexer_getters.md:84:- Drives: top HUD bar, season clock, winter indicator, leaderboard panel, bandit summary
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7234:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:50:Before bandit target selection is computed in a region, **all clans with homebases in the bandit's current region must be eagerly settled to that tick**.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7235:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:52:This requirement exists because bandit targeting depends on each base's current vault loot-value, and accurate loot-value requires up-to-date clan settlement.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7245:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:11:The phase ships ~85% of the bandit subsystem (state machine, spawn, attack resolution, defender split, blueprint reward, target-death cleanup). All five reviewers independently flagged the SAME critical gap: **production bandits never attack**. They spawn → Camped → STUCK FOREVER. All the attack/loot/blueprint code is dead in the live heartbeat path; only test harnesses force the Camped→Attacking transition.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7246:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:21:| H1 | `ClanWorld.sol:1551, 1654, 1679` | 5.4 + 5.5 + 4.7 + Gemini = **4/5** | HIGH | **Camped→Attacking dispatch missing in production heartbeat.** `_transitionBanditToAttacking` only called from test harnesses. `_advanceBanditStates` only handles Spawned→Camped + Resting→Camped. As merged, real bandits spawn, camp, and stay stuck. Attack resolution / loot split / blueprint reward / target death cleanup are all DEAD CODE in the live path. |
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7247:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:24:| H4 | `ClanWorld.sol:1755` + `1005-1016` | 5.4 = **1/5** | HIGH | **Blueprint reward unit mismatch.** Bandit defeat awards `targetClan.blueprintBalance += 1`. Monument upgrades consume `1e18` blueprint units. So one bandit win = 1 wei of blueprint. The new test at `BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:252-261` LOCKS THE BUG IN. Fix: award `1e18` and update test/event expectations. |
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7249:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:34:| M2 | `ClanWorld.sol:~525` (`_markClanDead`) | 4.7 = **1/5** | MED | **`reason` parameter swallowed.** Caller passes "starvation"/"bandit"/"unknown" but `ClanEliminated` event has no cause field. Either add cause field to event, or remove the parameter. |
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7250:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:36:| M4 | `ClanWorld.sol:~1813-1830` (`_pickBanditClansmanVictim`) | 4.7 = **1/5** | MED | **Victim pool too broad.** A clansman fishing in REGION_DEEP_SEA can die when bandit attacks home base. Confirm intent vs. restrict pool to base-region defenders. |
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7251:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:52:1. **The Phase delivers ~85% of stated bandit goal.** State machine, spawn, eager-settle, attack resolution mechanics, defender split, blueprint reward, target-death cleanup — all present and individually tested. The missing 15% is the production wiring of Camped→Attacking dispatch + escape→rest recovery. This is the H1 finding.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7279:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v1_implementation_profile.md:315:5. bandits threatening a base
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7282:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:5:**Scope:** World layout, tick semantics, settlement rules, missions, resources, market execution, bandits, winter, building, victory, human steering, trust model, spawn rules, elimination rules, pool seeding, and season lifecycle.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7319:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:694:This tiebreak is deterministic and prevents bandit targeting from stalling or becoming ambiguous when multiple bases have identical vault values.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7354:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:947:- walls may lose levels from winter cold damage and bandit pressure
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7355:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:948:- base levels do not lose levels from bandits in v1
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7403:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:933:- only vault resources count for upkeep, winter burn, building, and bandit target value
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7420:docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_numbered_implementation_plan.md:363:**Cut line:** blueprint gating can be deferred temporarily if bandit loop is not ready yet.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7789:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2419:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-synthesis.md:18:**Single-model new HIGH (Codex 5.4 only):** `_pickBanditAttackTarget` ranks competing targets from RAW unsettled clan state. Eager-settle only covers spawn-candidate regions, skipped at global bandit cap. Multi-clan target selection can pick the wrong clan based on stale loot/liveness in production.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8006:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2870:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:2022:+            emit WallDamagedByBandit(clanId, clan.wallLevel, banditId);
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8045:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3066:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8650:The direct fix targets look clean: `_applyUpkeep()` and `_simulateApplyUpkeep()` use `_isWinterTick(tick)`, blueprint reward uses `BLUEPRINT_UNIT`, and `_resolveBanditAttack()` re-checks bandit state/target after settlement.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8046:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3070:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8681:The direct fix targets look clean: `_applyUpkeep()` and `_simulateApplyUpkeep()` use `_isWinterTick(tick)`, blueprint reward uses `BLUEPRINT_UNIT`, and `_resolveBanditAttack()` re-checks bandit state/target after settlement.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8148:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3451:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7727:  1905	            emit WallDamagedByBandit(clanId, clan.wallLevel, banditId);
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8174:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3646:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7385:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:21:| H1 | `ClanWorld.sol:1551, 1654, 1679` | 5.4 + 5.5 + 4.7 + Gemini = **4/5** | HIGH | **Camped→Attacking dispatch missing in production heartbeat.** `_transitionBanditToAttacking` only called from test harnesses. `_advanceBanditStates` only handles Spawned→Camped + Resting→Camped. As merged, real bandits spawn, camp, and stay stuck. Attack resolution / loot split / blueprint reward / target death cleanup are all DEAD CODE in the live path. |
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8185:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3680:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:6:**NEEDS_FIXES.** The phase ships a mostly-coherent bandit subsystem, but it has a critical architectural gap: bandits **never actually attack in production**. There is code for spawn, camp transition, attack resolution, defender split, blueprint reward, and target-death cleanup — but nothing in `heartbeat()` ever transitions a Camped bandit into the Attacking state, so all of `_resolveAttackingBandits` / `_resolveBanditAttack` / loot / blueprint / cleanup logic is unreachable from a normal heartbeat. The defender registry, simulation parity, seed timing, and arithmetic all look sound otherwise. Recommend a single follow-up fix to wire Camped→Attacking dispatch (or explicit acknowledgment that Wave-N attack dispatch is deferred to a sub-issue) before merging.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8189:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3689:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:61:1. **Phase delivers ~85% of the stated bandit goal.** State machine ✓, spawn ✓, eager-settle ✓, attack resolution mechanics ✓, defender split ✓, blueprint reward ✓, target-death cleanup ✓. Missing piece: target-selection + Camped→Attacking dispatch in production heartbeat (H1). All six newly added Solidity events for the attack lifecycle (`BanditTargetDied`, `WallDamagedByBandit`, `ClansmanKilledByBandit`, `BlueprintEarned`, `LootDistributed`) are unreachable until that gap closes — Convex indexer wiring for those events is therefore untested in integration.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8190:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3692:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8193:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3703:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:482:The wall damage calculation restricts the maximum damage absorbed to exactly `WALL_HP_PER_LEVEL` (100) per attack, regardless of `clan.wallLevel`. Any incoming damage exceeding 100 instantly spills over to the base and clansmen, and the wall only loses exactly 1 level. This means a fully upgraded Level 5 wall offers no more immediate damage mitigation against a large bandit attack than a Level 1 wall, heavily undermining the defensive value of upgrading walls.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8195:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3705:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:487:The `_simulateSettleToTick` function simulates individual clans in isolation. If a clansman is on a `DefendBase` mission and the target clan is eliminated by bandits during the unsettled window, the real `_markClanDead` correctly releases those defenders. However, the view-only simulation has no visibility into the target clan's death and will incorrectly mock the clansman as `ACTING` (defending) indefinitely. This will cause `getClanFullView` and the UI to show clansmen stuck defending a dead base until their own clan is explicitly settled.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8196:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3710:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:11:The phase ships ~85% of the bandit subsystem (state machine, spawn, attack resolution, defender split, blueprint reward, target-death cleanup). All five reviewers independently flagged the SAME critical gap: **production bandits never attack**. They spawn → Camped → STUCK FOREVER. All the attack/loot/blueprint code is dead in the live heartbeat path; only test harnesses force the Camped→Attacking transition.

As merged, real bandits spawn, camp, and stay stuck. Attack resolution / loot split / blueprint reward / target death cleanup are all DEAD CODE in the live path. |

Monument upgrades consume `1e18` blueprint units. So one bandit win = 1 wei of blueprint. The new test at `BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:252-261` LOCKS THE BUG IN. Fix: award `1e18` and update test/event expectations. |

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8269:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3966:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7812:packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:290:        emit BlueprintEarned(clanId, banditId, world.blueprintUnit(), world.getWorldState().currentTick);
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8270:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3992:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8295:  Heartbeat now correctly drives `Camped -> Attacking -> resolution`, but `_pickBanditAttackTarget()` still ranks candidates from raw `_clans` storage without first settling all competing clans to `closedTick`. The new eager-settle step only settles spawn-candidate regions and is skipped entirely at the global bandit cap, so live attacks can choose the wrong target based on stale loot/liveness, then resolve that wrong attack normally. The new end-to-end heartbeat test proves the lifecycle is live, but it only uses a single clan, so this competitive-selection bug is still untested.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8271:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3998:docs/reviews/pr194-r2-codereview-codex-5-4.md:8331:  Heartbeat now correctly drives `Camped -> Attacking -> resolution`, but `_pickBanditAttackTarget()` still ranks candidates from raw `_clans` storage without first settling all competing clans to `closedTick`. The new end-to-end heartbeat test proves the lifecycle is live, but it only uses a single clan, so this competitive-selection bug is still untested.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8395:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4493:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14959:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1755` and `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1005-1016` — Blueprint rewards use the wrong unit. Bandit defeat credits `targetClan.blueprintBalance += 1`, but monument upgrades consume `1e18` blueprint units. That makes one bandit win worth one wei of blueprint, and the new test in `packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:252-261` locks that bug in. Suggested fix: award `1e18` per blueprint reward and update the event/test expectations to the same unit.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8396:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4497:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:15001:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1755` and `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1005-1016` — Blueprint rewards use the wrong unit. That makes one bandit win worth one wei of blueprint, and the new test in `packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:252-261` locks that bug in. Suggested fix: award `1e18` per blueprint reward and update the event/test expectations to the same unit.

Eager-settle only covers spawn-candidate regions, skipped at global bandit cap. Multi-clan target selection can pick the wrong clan based on stale loot/liveness in production.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8857:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14959:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1755` and `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1005-1016` — Blueprint rewards use the wrong unit. Bandit defeat credits `targetClan.blueprintBalance += 1`, but monument upgrades consume `1e18` blueprint units. That makes one bandit win worth one wei of blueprint, and the new test in `packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:252-261` locks that bug in. Suggested fix: award `1e18` per blueprint reward and update the event/test expectations to the same unit.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8859:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14982:- The integrated head tree has drifted materially from the documented bandit design: combat is now randomized per-defender roll, reward split is clan-based, legacy fields are partially stubbed, and there is still no heartbeat-driven target-selection/rampage path. The new tests mostly validate internal helpers via harnesses instead of proving the production heartbeat can drive the full game loop.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8861:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:15001:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1755` and `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1005-1016` — Blueprint rewards use the wrong unit. Bandit defeat credits `targetClan.blueprintBalance += 1`, but monument upgrades consume `1e18` blueprint units. That makes one bandit win worth one wei of blueprint, and the new test in `packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:252-261` locks that bug in. Suggested fix: award `1e18` per blueprint reward and update the event/test expectations to the same unit.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:12339:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3594:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10599:`packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1654` — Camped bandits never select a target or transition to `Attacking` in production. `_transitionBanditToAttacking` exists at `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1551`, but only harness tests call it; heartbeat `_advanceBanditStates` only handles `Spawned -> Camped` and `Resting -> Camped`, so `_resolveAttackingBandits` at `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1679` never runs for real spawned bandits. Add deterministic target selection for eligible camped bandits, call `_transitionBanditToAttacking`, and add an end-to-end spawn-to-attack heartbeat test without harness state mutation.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:12340:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3595:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10605:`packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1717` — `_resolveBanditAttack` can revert if `_settleClan(targetClanId)` kills the target. Target death calls `_markClanDead`, which aborts attacking bandits for that target at `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:576`; then `_resolveBanditAttack` continues and may try to transition the already-`Escaped` bandit again at `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1758`, which is invalid. Re-check `targetClan.clanState` and `bandit.state/targetClanId` after target and defender settlement, returning if cleanup already handled the bandit.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:12341:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3597:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10641:`packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1654` — Camped bandits never select a target or transition to `Attacking` in production. `_transitionBanditToAttacking` exists at `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1551`, but only harness tests call it; heartbeat `_advanceBanditStates` only handles `Spawned -> Camped` and `Resting -> Camped`, so `_resolveAttackingBandits` at `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1679` never runs for real spawned bandits. Add deterministic target selection for eligible camped bandits, call `_transitionBanditToAttacking`, and add an end-to-end spawn-to-attack heartbeat test without harness state mutation.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:12342:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3598:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10647:`packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1717` — `_resolveBanditAttack` can revert if `_settleClan(targetClanId)` kills the target. Target death calls `_markClanDead`, which aborts attacking bandits for that target at `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:576`; then `_resolveBanditAttack` continues and may try to transition the already-`Escaped` bandit again at `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1758`, which is invalid. Re-check `targetClan.clanState` and `bandit.state/targetClanId` after target and defender settlement, returning if cleanup already handled the bandit.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:12344:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3682:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:11:`packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1551` (`_transitionBanditToAttacking`) is **only called from test harnesses** (`Bandit.t.sol:18`, `BanditAttackResolution.t.sol`, `BanditSpawn.t.sol`). The heartbeat steps 4–6 (`_advanceBanditStates`, `_resolveAttackingBandits`, `_evaluateBanditSpawns`, `ClanWorld.sol:2243-2252`) only handle Spawned→Camped, Resting→Camped, attack-resolution-of-already-Attacking, and new spawns. There is no target-selection + state-transition step for Camped bandits, so on the live engine bandits spawn, transition to Camped, and remain there forever. Consequently `_resolveAttackingBandits`, `_resolveBanditAttack`, `_distributeBanditLootToDefendingClans`, `BlueprintEarned`, `_releaseDefendersForDeadTarget`, `_abortBanditAttacksForDeadTarget`, and the `BanditTargetDied` event are **dead code in the live heartbeat path**. The PR description claims "Pending sub-issues: 9.4 Deterministic attack resolution," but the merged commit `d02061f feat(contracts): deterministic bandit attack resolution (#207)` is part of this PR — so the gap is dispatch wiring, not resolution logic. **Suggested fix:** add a step between `_advanceBanditStates` and `_resolveAttackingBandits` that, for each Camped bandit whose camp window has elapsed, picks a target via `_banditSpawnRegionWeights`-style scoring (or the loot-value heuristic in `_lootValueRaw`) and calls `_transitionBanditToAttacking`. Alternatively, file a follow-up issue and add a top-of-file comment in the heartbeat docstring explicitly marking attack dispatch as deferred so reviewers and the Convex indexer don't assume the system is live.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:12345:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3692:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:12346:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3716:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:52:1. **The Phase delivers ~85% of stated bandit goal.** State machine, spawn, eager-settle, attack resolution mechanics, defender split, blueprint reward, target-death cleanup — all present and individually tested. The missing 15% is the production wiring of Camped→Attacking dispatch + escape→rest recovery. This is the H1 finding.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:12369:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4414:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:13885:23f7f1a:packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1686:                bool shouldResolve = bandit.state == BanditState.Attacking && bandit.tickEnteredState == closedTick;
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:12370:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4415:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:13886:23f7f1a:packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1702:        if (bandit.id == ClanWorldConstants.BANDIT_ID_NULL || bandit.state != BanditState.Attacking) {
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:12371:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4492:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14955:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1551, 1654, 1679` — The state machine is incomplete in production. Heartbeat advances `Spawned -> Camped` and `Resting -> Camped`, but there is no non-test caller for `_transitionBanditToAttacking`, and no live caller ever moves `Escaped -> Resting`. The tests mask this by forcing state transitions through harness-only helpers in `packages/contracts/test/Bandit.t.sol` and `packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol`. As merged, real bandits never attack, and any escaped bandit would permanently occupy active/cap slots. Suggested fix: wire heartbeat-driven target selection/rampage transitions into the live contract and add at least one end-to-end test from natural spawn through attack and post-attack recovery.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:12372:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4493:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14959:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1755` and `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1005-1016` — Blueprint rewards use the wrong unit. Bandit defeat credits `targetClan.blueprintBalance += 1`, but monument upgrades consume `1e18` blueprint units. That makes one bandit win worth one wei of blueprint, and the new test in `packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:252-261` locks that bug in. Suggested fix: award `1e18` per blueprint reward and update the event/test expectations to the same unit.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:12375:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4496:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14997:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1551, 1654, 1679` — The state machine is incomplete in production. Heartbeat advances `Spawned -> Camped` and `Resting -> Camped`, but there is no non-test caller for `_transitionBanditToAttacking`, and no live caller ever moves `Escaped -> Resting`. The tests mask this by forcing state transitions through harness-only helpers in `packages/contracts/test/Bandit.t.sol` and `packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol`. As merged, real bandits never attack, and any escaped bandit would permanently occupy active/cap slots. Suggested fix: wire heartbeat-driven target selection/rampage transitions into the live contract and add at least one end-to-end test from natural spawn through attack and post-attack recovery.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:12376:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:4497:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:15001:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1755` and `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1005-1016` — Blueprint rewards use the wrong unit. Bandit defeat credits `targetClan.blueprintBalance += 1`, but monument upgrades consume `1e18` blueprint units. That makes one bandit win worth one wei of blueprint, and the new test in `packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:252-261` locks that bug in. Suggested fix: award `1e18` per blueprint reward and update the event/test expectations to the same unit.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:12516:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5648:docs/reviews/pr198-synthesis.md:11:The market plumbing is structurally sound (immediate + scheduled FIFO, k-invariant AMM, heartbeat ordering preserved, 116/116 tests). All 3 reviewers independently flag the same fundamental finding: **market trades operate on the clan vault, not worker carry — violating spec §8.3-8.4.** The impl + tests are mutually consistent but the spec contract is broken. Implications cascade into bandit loot timing + physical-presence game design.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:12520:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6250:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10325:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1656`, `1771`, `1824`, `1946` — Market trades bypass clansman carry and mutate clan vaults directly. Spec says `MarketSell` source is worker carry and `MarketBuy` destination is worker carry (`docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:565-585`). Current code lets a worker in Unicorn Town sell homebase vault resources without carrying them, and bought resources appear in the vault without a return/deposit trip. This also changes bandit loot/target timing because vault value changes immediately.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:12521:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6252:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-5.md:10360:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1656`, `1771`, `1824`, `1946` — Market trades bypass clansman carry and mutate clan vaults directly. Spec says `MarketSell` source is worker carry and `MarketBuy` destination is worker carry (`docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:565-585`). Current code lets a worker in Unicorn Town sell homebase vault resources without carrying them, and bought resources appear in the vault without a return/deposit trip. This also changes bandit loot/target timing because vault value changes immediately.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:12526:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6929:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:12533:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7251:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:52:1. **The Phase delivers ~85% of stated bandit goal.** State machine, spawn, eager-settle, attack resolution mechanics, defender split, blueprint reward, target-death cleanup — all present and individually tested. The missing 15% is the production wiring of Camped→Attacking dispatch + escape→rest recovery. This is the H1 finding.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:12582:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8190:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3692:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-6.md:11:Phase 9 delivers the bandit state machine (spawn, camp, attack, defeat/escape cycle), deterministic attack resolution, loot distribution, and a significant refactor of the heartbeat seed timing. The state machine is well-structured and the heartbeat ordering is correct. One HIGH-severity bug exists in `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate` using the wrong seed for its pre-spawn candidacy check, which causes eager-settle to diverge from the actual spawn decision. Four MEDIUM-severity issues affect the `getClanFullView` defender display, simulation starvation check, wall damage accounting, and missing integration test coverage. **Merge is safe after the HIGH fix; MEDIUMs can be follow-up issues.**

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:13148:A useful wrinkle surfaced: this branch is stacked on Phase 5, not the later Phase 9 bandit implementation, so any “Phase 9 interaction” finding has to be phrased as an integration risk against the known blueprint/wall contract, not as code present in this checkout. I’m checking the queued-up subreviews now and then I’ll collapse this into the requested final format.

docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4465:Reviewing the Phase 9 bandit diff end-to-end now. I’m going to trace the contract state machine, heartbeat ordering, and test coverage in parallel so we can catch integration bugs instead of just local issues.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4859:///         Phase 2 (market execution) and Phase 3 (bandits, winter damage) are stubbed.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5557:        // TODO Phase 3: bandit state transitions and attacks
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5741:I’ve got the baseline contract shape loaded now. Next I’m splitting the review into three parallel passes: bandit lifecycle + heartbeat, derived-settlement simulation, and ABI/test/integration seams so we can compare conclusions instead of trusting one path.

docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11735:./docs/adr/ADR001-IClanWorld-as-web2-web3-integration-seam.md:5:	1.	I made BanditState.NONE = 0 so bandit.state == NONE is the natural “no active bandit” check. v4.2 omitted this; adding it avoids the ambiguous default value.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11750:./docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:38:**Assert:** Either (a) no bandit spawns (spawn skipped due to excluded region) OR (b) bandit's `currentRegion` is NOT Unicorn Town and NOT Deep Sea.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11754:./docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:72:**Setup:** Spawn bandit at tick N with `nextActionTick = N + 3`.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11755:./docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:82:**Setup:** Spawn bandit at tick N, `nextActionTick = N + 2`. Mint a clan in the bandit's region (so target selection can succeed).
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11757:./docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:205:- Register N defenders at defender clan's base; their combined `defensePoints` > bandit's `attackPower`.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11760:./docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:289:**Setup:** Resolve one bandit attack. Read `defenseContributionsByBanditId[banditId]`.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11763:./docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:460:- `defenseContributionsByBanditId[banditId]` empty after resolution.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11764:./docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:475:**Fuzzer:** For any spawned bandit, `BanditTroop.currentRegion != REGION_UNICORN_TOWN && != REGION_DEEP_SEA`.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:11765:./docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:502:- The `_eagerSettleForBandit(banditId)` call MUST happen before target selection AND before damage application (3.E2). Tests 3.E2-a and 3.E2-b directly verify this ordering.

docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:13920:23f7f1a:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:48:## A4. Eager-settle scope for bandit target selection
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:13921:23f7f1a:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:50:Before bandit target selection is computed in a region, **all clans with homebases in the bandit's current region must be eagerly settled to that tick**.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:13924:23f7f1a:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:65:Before selecting a bandit target in region `R`, the engine must eagerly settle:
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:13926:23f7f1a:docs/planning/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:620:function getBanditTargetPreview(uint32 banditId) external view returns (uint32 previewClanId);
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:13933:23f7f1a:docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:82:**Setup:** Spawn bandit at tick N, `nextActionTick = N + 2`. Mint a clan in the bandit's region (so target selection can succeed).
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:13934:23f7f1a:docs/planning/phase-3-test-spec.md:502:- The `_eagerSettleForBandit(banditId)` call MUST happen before target selection AND before damage application (3.E2). Tests 3.E2-a and 3.E2-b directly verify this ordering.
