docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14647:One of the parallel passes finished and it confirmed the biggest concern I was already seeing: the production heartbeat never drives the full bandit lifecycle end-to-end, while the tests mostly do it through harness-only shortcuts. I’m waiting on the simulation/API passes so we can merge the findings cleanly instead of duplicating them.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14890:   457	    uint32 banditId;
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14952:NEEDS_FIXES. The biggest blocker is that the production heartbeat still does not drive the full bandit lifecycle: real bandits can spawn and camp, but there is no live path to target selection/attack, and escaped bandits never re-enter the cycle. There are also two deploy-safety issues outside the contract core: winter starvation replay is wrong for historically-settled ticks, and the repo still contains stale handwritten ABIs that will misdecode the new `WorldState` shape.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14955:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1551, 1654, 1679` — The state machine is incomplete in production. Heartbeat advances `Spawned -> Camped` and `Resting -> Camped`, but there is no non-test caller for `_transitionBanditToAttacking`, and no live caller ever moves `Escaped -> Resting`. The tests mask this by forcing state transitions through harness-only helpers in `packages/contracts/test/Bandit.t.sol` and `packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol`. As merged, real bandits never attack, and any escaped bandit would permanently occupy active/cap slots. Suggested fix: wire heartbeat-driven target selection/rampage transitions into the live contract and add at least one end-to-end test from natural spawn through attack and post-attack recovery.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14959:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1755` and `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1005-1016` — Blueprint rewards use the wrong unit. Bandit defeat credits `targetClan.blueprintBalance += 1`, but monument upgrades consume `1e18` blueprint units. That makes one bandit win worth one wei of blueprint, and the new test in `packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:252-261` locks that bug in. Suggested fix: award `1e18` per blueprint reward and update the event/test expectations to the same unit.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14972:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1548, 3473-3503` and `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:455-473` — Several legacy bandit-facing fields are still exposed but now carry placeholder zeroes (`tier`, `attackAttemptsMade`, `maxAttemptsRemaining`, `projectedTargetLootValue`). That silently degrades downstream behavior instead of failing loudly. Suggested fix: either compute real values or version/remove the legacy fields and update consumers.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14977:- `packages/shared/src/mocks/clanWorldFixture.ts:76-92, 309-317` — The shared mock/demo bandit schema is still pre-Phase-9 (`CAMPING`, `tier`, old field names). That will drift further from the contract seam even if Solidity-side fixes land.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14982:- The integrated head tree has drifted materially from the documented bandit design: combat is now randomized per-defender roll, reward split is clan-based, legacy fields are partially stubbed, and there is still no heartbeat-driven target-selection/rampage path. The new tests mostly validate internal helpers via harnesses instead of proving the production heartbeat can drive the full game loop.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14994:NEEDS_FIXES. The biggest blocker is that the production heartbeat still does not drive the full bandit lifecycle: real bandits can spawn and camp, but there is no live path to target selection/attack, and escaped bandits never re-enter the cycle. There are also two deploy-safety issues outside the contract core: winter starvation replay is wrong for historically-settled ticks, and the repo still contains stale handwritten ABIs that will misdecode the new `WorldState` shape.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14997:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1551, 1654, 1679` — The state machine is incomplete in production. Heartbeat advances `Spawned -> Camped` and `Resting -> Camped`, but there is no non-test caller for `_transitionBanditToAttacking`, and no live caller ever moves `Escaped -> Resting`. The tests mask this by forcing state transitions through harness-only helpers in `packages/contracts/test/Bandit.t.sol` and `packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol`. As merged, real bandits never attack, and any escaped bandit would permanently occupy active/cap slots. Suggested fix: wire heartbeat-driven target selection/rampage transitions into the live contract and add at least one end-to-end test from natural spawn through attack and post-attack recovery.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:15001:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1755` and `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1005-1016` — Blueprint rewards use the wrong unit. Bandit defeat credits `targetClan.blueprintBalance += 1`, but monument upgrades consume `1e18` blueprint units. That makes one bandit win worth one wei of blueprint, and the new test in `packages/contracts/test/BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:252-261` locks that bug in. Suggested fix: award `1e18` per blueprint reward and update the event/test expectations to the same unit.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:15014:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1548, 3473-3503` and `packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:455-473` — Several legacy bandit-facing fields are still exposed but now carry placeholder zeroes (`tier`, `attackAttemptsMade`, `maxAttemptsRemaining`, `projectedTargetLootValue`). That silently degrades downstream behavior instead of failing loudly. Suggested fix: either compute real values or version/remove the legacy fields and update consumers.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:15019:- `packages/shared/src/mocks/clanWorldFixture.ts:76-92, 309-317` — The shared mock/demo bandit schema is still pre-Phase-9 (`CAMPING`, `tier`, old field names). That will drift further from the contract seam even if Solidity-side fixes land.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:15024:- The integrated head tree has drifted materially from the documented bandit design: combat is now randomized per-defender roll, reward split is clan-based, legacy fields are partially stubbed, and there is still no heartbeat-driven target-selection/rampage path. The new tests mostly validate internal helpers via harnesses instead of proving the production heartbeat can drive the full game loop.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:464:VERDICT: **NEEDS_FIXES**. While the Phase 9 PR successfully integrates foundational spawn chance logic and deterministic attack resolution, it suffers from critical architectural drift regarding the bandit state machine and eager-settlement scope. Bandits lack automated transitions to select targets or recover from escapes, rendering the threat entirely inert in practice. Additionally, wall defense calculations and view-only settlement simulations contain edge cases that undermine base progression and UI accuracy. 
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:470:Although sub-issues 9.1 and 9.4 outline a complete state machine, the actual automated transitions driving it are missing. There is no logic in `_advanceBanditStates()` to transition a bandit from `Camped` to `Attacking` (Target Selection is completely unimplemented). Furthermore, when an attack resolves without a defeat and a bandit transitions to `Escaped`, there is no logic to transition it back to `Resting`. As a result, once spawned, a bandit will get permanently stuck in `Camped` (or, if forcefully triggered, stuck in `Escaped`).
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:471:*Suggested fix:* Implement target selection logic in `_advanceBanditStates()` to evaluate eligible `Camped` bandits, pick a valid target, and call `_transitionBanditToAttacking()`. Additionally, add a transition from `Escaped` to `Resting` either immediately during attack resolution or as an automated step in `_advanceBanditStates()`.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:473:**Eager settlement completely skips target selection requirements for existing bandits**
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:475:Sub-issue 9.3 mandates eager-settling bases and defenders "before target selection" to ensure targets are chosen based on fresh, up-to-date loot values. However, `_eagerSettleForBandits()` includes an early return `if (_activeBanditCount >= MAX_TOTAL_BANDITS) return;`, completely bypassing all settlement when the global bandit cap is reached. Furthermore, it only settles regions that pass `_isBanditSpawnRegionCandidate()`—ignoring regions that have existing `Camped` bandits waiting to select targets but aren't currently eligible for a *new* spawn. This guarantees stale target evaluation.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:476:*Suggested fix:* Remove the early return for `MAX_TOTAL_BANDITS`. Adjust the region loop to eager-settle any region that either is a spawn candidate OR contains a `Camped` bandit ready to perform target selection.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:482:The wall damage calculation restricts the maximum damage absorbed to exactly `WALL_HP_PER_LEVEL` (100) per attack, regardless of `clan.wallLevel`. Any incoming damage exceeding 100 instantly spills over to the base and clansmen, and the wall only loses exactly 1 level. This means a fully upgraded Level 5 wall offers no more immediate damage mitigation against a large bandit attack than a Level 1 wall, heavily undermining the defensive value of upgrading walls.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:487:The `_simulateSettleToTick` function simulates individual clans in isolation. If a clansman is on a `DefendBase` mission and the target clan is eliminated by bandits during the unsettled window, the real `_markClanDead` correctly releases those defenders. However, the view-only simulation has no visibility into the target clan's death and will incorrectly mock the clansman as `ACTING` (defending) indefinitely. This will cause `getClanFullView` and the UI to show clansmen stuck defending a dead base until their own clan is explicitly settled.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:494:The `BanditAttackResolved` event emits hardcoded `0`s for the `stolenWood`, `stolenIron`, `stolenWheat`, and `stolenFish` fields even if the bandit escapes successfully. If resource stealing is deferred to a future phase, it should be documented as a stub; otherwise, the bandit should actually drain resources from the target clan's vault upon an un-defeated attack.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:4:**Phase:** dev-phase-9-bandits — Bandit System
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:11:The phase ships ~85% of the bandit subsystem (state machine, spawn, attack resolution, defender split, blueprint reward, target-death cleanup). All five reviewers independently flagged the SAME critical gap: **production bandits never attack**. They spawn → Camped → STUCK FOREVER. All the attack/loot/blueprint code is dead in the live heartbeat path; only test harnesses force the Camped→Attacking transition.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:21:| H1 | `ClanWorld.sol:1551, 1654, 1679` | 5.4 + 5.5 + 4.7 + Gemini = **4/5** | HIGH | **Camped→Attacking dispatch missing in production heartbeat.** `_transitionBanditToAttacking` only called from test harnesses. `_advanceBanditStates` only handles Spawned→Camped + Resting→Camped. As merged, real bandits spawn, camp, and stay stuck. Attack resolution / loot split / blueprint reward / target death cleanup are all DEAD CODE in the live path. |
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:24:| H4 | `ClanWorld.sol:1755` + `1005-1016` | 5.4 = **1/5** | HIGH | **Blueprint reward unit mismatch.** Bandit defeat awards `targetClan.blueprintBalance += 1`. Monument upgrades consume `1e18` blueprint units. So one bandit win = 1 wei of blueprint. The new test at `BanditAttackResolution.t.sol:252-261` LOCKS THE BUG IN. Fix: award `1e18` and update test/event expectations. |
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:25:| H5 | `ClanWorld.sol:1717` | 5.5 = **1/5** | HIGH | **`_resolveBanditAttack` reverts on target-death-during-attack.** `_settleClan(targetClanId)` may kill the target → `_markClanDead` aborts the attacking bandit → state machine then tries to transition already-`Escaped` bandit again at `:1758`, which is invalid. Race window. Fix: re-check `targetClan.clanState` and `bandit.state/targetClanId` after target/defender settlement; return if cleanup already handled. |
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:34:| M2 | `ClanWorld.sol:~525` (`_markClanDead`) | 4.7 = **1/5** | MED | **`reason` parameter swallowed.** Caller passes "starvation"/"bandit"/"unknown" but `ClanEliminated` event has no cause field. Either add cause field to event, or remove the parameter. |
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:36:| M4 | `ClanWorld.sol:~1813-1830` (`_pickBanditClansmanVictim`) | 4.7 = **1/5** | MED | **Victim pool too broad.** A clansman fishing in REGION_DEEP_SEA can die when bandit attacks home base. Confirm intent vs. restrict pool to base-region defenders. |
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:52:1. **The Phase delivers ~85% of stated bandit goal.** State machine, spawn, eager-settle, attack resolution mechanics, defender split, blueprint reward, target-death cleanup — all present and individually tested. The missing 15% is the production wiring of Camped→Attacking dispatch + escape→rest recovery. This is the H1 finding.

docs/reviews/pr200-codereview-codex-5-4.md:4717:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7390:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:19:`ClanWorld.sol:~2210-2230` documents the heartbeat as 5 steps ("1. Settle… 2. Execute markets… 3. Eager-settle… 4. Advance bandit timers and resolve closed-tick bandit/world events… 5. Increment tick and publish the next tick seed atomically"), but the body has 8 inline-numbered steps (`Step 1`…`Step 8`), bandit spawn evaluation is its own step, and `_resolveWorldEvents` runs at step 7 not step 4. Stale natspec → reviewer/operator confusion. Trivial fix.

Increment tick and publish the next tick seed atomically"), but the body has 8 inline-numbered steps (`Step 1`…`Step 8`), bandit spawn evaluation is its own step, and `_resolveWorldEvents` runs at step 7 not step 4. Stale natspec → reviewer/operator confusion. Trivial fix.

docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:6:**NEEDS_FIXES.** The phase ships a mostly-coherent bandit subsystem, but it has a critical architectural gap: bandits **never actually attack in production**. There is code for spawn, camp transition, attack resolution, defender split, blueprint reward, and target-death cleanup — but nothing in `heartbeat()` ever transitions a Camped bandit into the Attacking state, so all of `_resolveAttackingBandits` / `_resolveBanditAttack` / loot / blueprint / cleanup logic is unreachable from a normal heartbeat. The defender registry, simulation parity, seed timing, and arithmetic all look sound otherwise. Recommend a single follow-up fix to wire Camped→Attacking dispatch (or explicit acknowledgment that Wave-N attack dispatch is deferred to a sub-issue) before merging.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:11:`packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1551` (`_transitionBanditToAttacking`) is **only called from test harnesses** (`Bandit.t.sol:18`, `BanditAttackResolution.t.sol`, `BanditSpawn.t.sol`). The heartbeat steps 4–6 (`_advanceBanditStates`, `_resolveAttackingBandits`, `_evaluateBanditSpawns`, `ClanWorld.sol:2243-2252`) only handle Spawned→Camped, Resting→Camped, attack-resolution-of-already-Attacking, and new spawns. There is no target-selection + state-transition step for Camped bandits, so on the live engine bandits spawn, transition to Camped, and remain there forever. Consequently `_resolveAttackingBandits`, `_resolveBanditAttack`, `_distributeBanditLootToDefendingClans`, `BlueprintEarned`, `_releaseDefendersForDeadTarget`, `_abortBanditAttacksForDeadTarget`, and the `BanditTargetDied` event are **dead code in the live heartbeat path**. The PR description claims "Pending sub-issues: 9.4 Deterministic attack resolution," but the merged commit `d02061f feat(contracts): deterministic bandit attack resolution (#207)` is part of this PR — so the gap is dispatch wiring, not resolution logic. **Suggested fix:** add a step between `_advanceBanditStates` and `_resolveAttackingBandits` that, for each Camped bandit whose camp window has elapsed, picks a target via `_banditSpawnRegionWeights`-style scoring (or the loot-value heuristic in `_lootValueRaw`) and calls `_transitionBanditToAttacking`. Alternatively, file a follow-up issue and add a top-of-file comment in the heartbeat docstring explicitly marking attack dispatch as deferred so reviewers and the Convex indexer don't assume the system is live.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:19:`ClanWorld.sol:~2210-2230` documents the heartbeat as 5 steps ("1. Settle… 2. Execute markets… 3. Eager-settle… 4. Advance bandit timers and resolve closed-tick bandit/world events… 5. Increment tick and publish the next tick seed atomically"), but the body has 8 inline-numbered steps (`Step 1`…`Step 8`), bandit spawn evaluation is its own step, and `_resolveWorldEvents` runs at step 7 not step 4. Stale natspec → reviewer/operator confusion. Trivial fix.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:22:`ClanWorld.sol:~525` overload is `function _markClanDead(uint32 clanId, string memory, uint64 tick, uint32 excludedBanditId)` — the named-by-position-only `string memory` argument is intentional (forge-lint workaround), but it means callers pass `"starvation"` / `"bandit"` / `"unknown"` and that information is dropped on the floor. The emitted `ClanEliminated(clanId, tick)` event has no cause field. If an operator wants to know whether a clan died from starvation vs. bandit, they must replay logs from preceding `BanditAttackResolved` / `ClansmanKilledByBandit` events. Either add a cause field to `ClanEliminated`, or remove the parameter entirely so the API doesn't pretend to track something it discards.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:25:`ClanWorld.sol:~1813-1830`: the victim pool is the entire `_clanClansmanIds[clanId]` filtered only by `state != DEAD`. A clansman fishing in REGION_DEEP_SEA can die when a bandit attacks the home base. This may be intentional ("base raid causes generalized casualties"), but it's a notable design choice that the spec extract in `docs/reference/architecture-decisions.md` should confirm. If not intentional, restrict the pool to `cs.currentRegion == clan.baseRegion` (or `cs.state == ClansmanState.WAITING`/`DefendBase`).
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:41:### L2. `_findOldestActiveBandit` definition of "oldest" is "lowest banditId"
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:42:`ClanWorld.sol:~1648-1664`: comment says "oldest active bandit" but the implementation returns the lowest live ID. That's actually the *first-spawned* (chronologically oldest), so the behavior matches the comment — but the description "oldest" without saying "by ID" is ambiguous. Single-sentence comment fix.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:51:`ClanWorld.sol:~3197-3199`: returns `_banditsByRegion[region]` directly. After deletes via `_deleteBandit`, the order changes due to swap-pop. Any UI that assumed insertion order will see flicker on bandit deaths. Document this or guarantee an order via a copy with a stable sort.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:53:### L6. `_canBanditLeaveSpawned` requires `currentTick >= tickEnteredState + 1` but `_advanceBanditStates` checks `closedTick > bandit.tickEnteredState`
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:57:`ClanWorld.sol:3195-3197` — `getBanditTroop(uint32 banditId) external view override returns (BanditTroop memory) { return getBandit(banditId); }`. ABI now has both. Pick one and keep it; the other is just a back-compat fossil with no production users in this branch.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:61:1. **Phase delivers ~85% of the stated bandit goal.** State machine ✓, spawn ✓, eager-settle ✓, attack resolution mechanics ✓, defender split ✓, blueprint reward ✓, target-death cleanup ✓. Missing piece: target-selection + Camped→Attacking dispatch in production heartbeat (H1). All six newly added Solidity events for the attack lifecycle (`BanditTargetDied`, `WallDamagedByBandit`, `ClansmanKilledByBandit`, `BlueprintEarned`, `LootDistributed`) are unreachable until that gap closes — Convex indexer wiring for those events is therefore untested in integration.

docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_convex_backend_spec.md:835:- Confirm whether the contract emits a `TickAdvanced` event from `heartbeat()` synchronously, before any other tick-end events. The indexer relies on `TickAdvanced` to trigger state-poll *after* all tick-end side effects have been written. If `TickAdvanced` fires *first* (before bandit attacks etc. for that tick), we either need to delay state-poll by a few ms or use a different trigger.

docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:48:## A4. Eager-settle scope for bandit target selection
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:50:Before bandit target selection is computed in a region, **all clans with homebases in the bandit's current region must be eagerly settled to that tick**.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:52:This requirement exists because bandit targeting depends on each base's current vault loot-value, and accurate loot-value requires up-to-date clan settlement.
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:103:- players are expected to maintain a real vault buffer rather than rely on same-tick rescue deliveries
docs/planning/clanworld_v4_1_addendum.md:182:When bandits are defeated and loot is dropped, the dropped portion is split **equally among all defending clansmen with nonzero defense contribution**, regardless of whether each qualifying defender contributed:

docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:495:If a clansman receives bandit-drop loot above carrying capacity:
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:614:- bandit defeat rewards
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:627:At most **one bandit troop** may be active in the world at a time.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:641:After a bandit troop is defeated or escapes:
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:643:- no new bandits may spawn during cooldown
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:647:- if no bandits spawn on a tick, spawn chance increases by `+1%`
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:649:- once bandits spawn, spawn chance resets and the cycle restarts after that troop is resolved
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:657:A bandit troop is always in one of:
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:668:After spawning, bandits camp in place for `3` ticks.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:689:When camp ends, or when bandits arrive in a new region during rampage:
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:694:This tiebreak is deterministic and prevents bandit targeting from stalling or becoming ambiguous when multiple bases have identical vault values.
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:717:- bandits enter `RESTING` state for `2` ticks
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:750:Working bandit attack tiers:
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:758:- early bandits are survivable with modest prep
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:759:- late bandits require active defense and/or mercenaries
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:764:If `clansmanDefense >= banditAttack`:
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:766:- bandits are defeated
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:771:If `clansmanDefense < banditAttack` and `totalDefense >= banditAttack`:
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:773:- bandits are defeated
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:778:If `totalDefense < banditAttack`:
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:780:- bandits steal `20%` of each vault physical resource:
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:787:- bandits remain alive and continue rampage after rest cycle
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:800:When bandits are defeated:
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:803:- `50%` of carried bandit loot is dropped
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:806:- the remaining `50%` of carried bandit loot is burned
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:947:- walls may lose levels from winter cold damage and bandit pressure
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:948:- base levels do not lose levels from bandits in v1

docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:1166:- at most one bandit troop is active at once
docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_spec.md:1192:- deterministic visible mutable bandit targeting

   190	// CORE STATE STRUCTS (raw storage shape)
   191	// =============================================================================
   192	
   193	struct WorldState {
   194	    uint64 currentTick;
   195	    uint64 seasonStartTick;
   196	    uint64 seasonEndTick;
   197	    bool seasonFinalized;
   198	    uint64 currentSeasonNumber; // 1-indexed; incremented each time seasonEndTick is crossed
   199	    uint64 nextHeartbeatAtTick; // estimated tick that will be opened by the next heartbeat (for off-chain UI)
   200	
   201	    uint64 nextHeartbeatAtTs;
   202	    uint64 nextBanditSpawnEligibleTick;
   203	    uint16 currentBanditSpawnChanceBps;
   204	    bytes32 currentTickSeed;
   205	
   206	    uint32 activeBanditId; // 0 if none
   207	    bool winterActive;
   208	    uint64 winterStartsAtTick;
   209	    uint64 winterEndsAtTick; // 0 if not active
   210	
   211	    uint64 nextCommitSequence; // global FIFO sequence for scheduled market actions
   212	}
   213	
   214	struct TreasuryState {
   215	    address treasuryOwner;
   216	    uint256 prizePotGold;
   217	
   218	    bool poolsSeeded;
   219	
   220	    address woodToken;
   221	    address wheatToken;
   222	    address fishToken;
   223	    address ironToken;
   224	    address goldToken;
   225	    address blueprintToken;
   226	
   227	    address woodGoldPool;
   228	    address wheatGoldPool;
   229	    address fishGoldPool;
   230	    address ironGoldPool;
   231	}
   232	
   233	struct Clan {
   234	    uint32 clanId;
   235	    uint256 iftTokenId;
   236	    address owner;
   237	    ClanState clanState;
   238	
   239	    uint8 baseRegion;
   240	    uint8 baseLevel;
   241	    uint8 wallLevel;
   242	    uint8 monumentLevel;
   243	    uint8 livingClansmen;
   244	
   245	    uint64 lastSettledTick;
   246	    uint64 starvationStartsAtTick; // 0 = none
   247	
   248	    uint16 coldDamage; // resets to 0 at winter end
   249	
   250	    uint256 goldBalance;
   251	    uint256 blueprintBalance;
   252	
   253	    uint256 vaultWood;
   254	    uint256 vaultIron;
   255	    uint256 vaultWheat;
   256	    uint256 vaultFish;
   257	}
   258	
   259	struct WheatPlot {
   260	    WheatPlotState state;

  2485	
  2486	    function test_nextHeartbeatAtTick_tracks_tick() public {
  2487	        WorldState memory ws0 = world.getWorldState();
  2488	        assertEq(ws0.nextHeartbeatAtTick, 1, "before first heartbeat, next = tick 1");
  2489	        vm.warp(block.timestamp + ClanWorldConstants.HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_SECONDS);
  2490	        world.heartbeat();
  2491	        WorldState memory ws1 = world.getWorldState();
  2492	        assertEq(ws1.currentTick, 1);
  2493	        assertEq(ws1.nextHeartbeatAtTick, 2, "after tick 1, next = tick 2");
  2494	    }
  2495	
  2496	    function test_marketSell_deductsFromCarry_notVault() public {
  2497	        // Use harness to directly set carry and test immediate sell
  2498	        ClanWorldTestHarness harness = new ClanWorldTestHarness();
  2499	        world = harness;
  2500	        _setupMarket();
  2501	        uint32 clanId = _mintClan();
  2502	        ClanFullView memory v = world.getClanFullView(clanId);
  2503	        uint32 csId = v.clansmen[0].clansman.clansman.clansmanId;
  2504	
  2505	        // Place worker at UT WAITING with carry wood
  2506	        harness.setClansmanForTest(csId, ClansmanState.WAITING, ClanWorldConstants.REGION_UNICORN_TOWN, 0);
  2507	        harness.setCarry(csId, 10e18, 0, 0, 0);
  2508	
  2509	        uint256 carryBefore = world.getClansman(csId).carryWood;
  2510	        uint256 vaultBefore = world.getClan(clanId).vaultWood;
  2511	        uint256 goldBefore = world.getClan(clanId).goldBalance;
  2512	
  2513	        address woodTok = world.getTreasuryState().woodToken;
  2514	        ClanOrder[] memory orders = new ClanOrder[](1);
  2515	        orders[0] = ClanOrder({
  2516	            clansmanId: csId,
  2517	            gotoRegion: ClanWorldConstants.REGION_UNICORN_TOWN,
  2518	            action: ActionType.MarketSell,
  2519	            targetClanId: 0,
  2520	            marketToken: woodTok,
  2521	            marketAmount: 5e18,
  2522	            maxGoldIn: 0
  2523	        });
  2524	        vm.prank(elder);
  2525	        OrderResult[] memory results = world.submitClanOrders(clanId, orders);
  2526	
  2527	        assertEq(uint8(results[0].status), uint8(StatusCode.OK), "immediate sell must succeed");
  2528	        assertEq(world.getClansman(csId).carryWood, carryBefore - 5e18, "sell: carry must decrease by sell amount");
  2529	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).vaultWood, vaultBefore, "sell: vault must be unchanged");
  2530	        assertGt(world.getClan(clanId).goldBalance, goldBefore, "sell: gold must increase");
  2531	    }
  2532	
  2533	    function test_marketSell_fails_emptyCarry_fullVault() public {
  2534	        // Setup: worker in UT with empty carry but vault has wood
  2535	        uint32 clanId = _mintClan();
  2536	        ClanFullView memory v = world.getClanFullView(clanId);
  2537	        uint32 csId = v.clansmen[0].clansman.clansman.clansmanId;
  2538	        // Advance cs to UT with no carry (no gather)
  2539	        _submitOrder(clanId, csId, ClanWorldConstants.REGION_UNICORN_TOWN, ActionType.Wait);
  2540	        (uint8 travelToUT,) = world.quoteTravel(v.clan.clan.baseRegion, ClanWorldConstants.REGION_UNICORN_TOWN);
  2541	        for (uint256 i = 0; i < uint256(travelToUT) + 2; i++) _advanceTick();
  2542	        world.settleClansman(csId);
  2543	        // Clan starts with vault wood from minting; carry is 0
  2544	        assertEq(world.getClansman(csId).carryWood, 0, "carry must be 0");
  2545	        assertGt(world.getClan(clanId).vaultWood, 0, "vault must have wood");
  2546	
  2547	        address woodTok = world.getTreasuryState().woodToken;
  2548	        ClanOrder[] memory orders = new ClanOrder[](1);
  2549	        orders[0] = ClanOrder({
  2550	            clansmanId: csId,
  2551	            gotoRegion: ClanWorldConstants.REGION_UNICORN_TOWN,
  2552	            action: ActionType.MarketSell,
  2553	            targetClanId: 0,
  2554	            marketToken: woodTok,
  2555	            marketAmount: 1e18,
  2556	            maxGoldIn: 0
  2557	        });
  2558	        vm.prank(elder);
  2559	        OrderResult[] memory results = world.submitClanOrders(clanId, orders);
  2560	        // Immediate path: carry empty → ERR_MISSING_RESOURCES or market failure
  2561	        // Either the order returns ERR_MISSING_RESOURCES at submit, or it's enqueued and fails at execution
  2562	        // With carry-based validation, immediate sell should fail
  2563	        assertNotEq(uint8(results[0].status), uint8(StatusCode.OK), "empty carry sell must not succeed");
  2564	    }
  2565	

codex
I’ve found one likely real regression already: the new “empty carry sell” test and the current market code disagree on behavior, which usually means either the fix is incomplete or the new test was written against a rule the contract still doesn’t enforce. I’m checking the surrounding API consumers now so we can separate contract bugs from test-only gaps.

docs/planning/battleplan-v1.txt:3:Doc’s there, ready to forward. The price-tracking idea is folded into getMarketState() — spot price computed onchain in 18-decimal fixed point, indexer stores one price row per resource per tick, UI gets free sparklines. No separate getter needed.
docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_frontend_spec.md:830:- Confirm `getMarketState().spotPriceGoldPerResource` is computed onchain. If not, frontend computes from reserves (cheap, fine).

/bin/bash -lc "nl -ba \"docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md\" | sed -n '750,775p'" in /home/claude/code/clan-world

   750	);
   751	```
   752	
   753	If target chain behavior differs, the implementation may swap to a chain-appropriate source later, but caller-supplied seed input is forbidden.
   754	
   755	### 11.2 Immediate vs scheduled market ordering
   756	Immediate market actions during tick `T` execute immediately in Elder tx order against current pool state at that tx’s execution time.
   757	
   758	Scheduled market actions for tick `T` execute later at the closing heartbeat of tick `T`, in ascending `commitSequence` order.
   759	
   760	Therefore:
   761	- immediate actions during tick `T` may front-run scheduled actions that will execute at the close of tick `T`
   762	
   763	### 11.3 Scheduled action lifecycle
   764	A scheduled market action:
   765	- is created when a travel-based market mission is accepted
   766	- is indexed under `executeAtTick`
   767	- executes eagerly at the corresponding heartbeat
   768	- is deleted from storage after success or failure resolution
   769	
   770	### 11.4 Reentrancy and interaction discipline
   771	Any function path that can trigger AMM interaction must follow checks-effects-interactions discipline.
   772	
   773	Recommended:
   774	- apply `nonReentrant` to immediate-order paths that can swap
   775	- apply `nonReentrant` to `heartbeat()` if it performs scheduled swaps

  1362	    // Test 15: scheduledMarket_fifo — same-tick sells execute in submission order
  1363	    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
  1364	
  1365	    function test_scheduledMarket_fifo() public {
  1366	        ClanWorldTestHarness harness = new ClanWorldTestHarness();
  1367	        world = harness;
  1368	        address woodAddr = _setupMarket();
  1369	        uint32 clanId = _mintClan();
  1370	        ClanFullView memory view_ = world.getClanFullView(clanId);
  1371	        // Give each clansman carry wood (must be >= their respective sell amount)
  1372	        for (uint256 i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
  1373	            harness.setCarry(view_.clansmen[i].clansman.clansman.clansmanId, 10e18, 0, 0, 0);
  1374	        }
  1375	
  1376	        ClanOrder[] memory orders = new ClanOrder[](3);
  1377	        for (uint256 i = 0; i < orders.length; i++) {
  1378	            orders[i] = ClanOrder({
  1379	                clansmanId: view_.clansmen[i].clansman.clansman.clansmanId,
  1380	                gotoRegion: ClanWorldConstants.REGION_UNICORN_TOWN,
  1381	                action: ActionType.MarketSell,
  1382	                targetClanId: 0,
  1383	                marketToken: woodAddr,
  1384	                marketAmount: uint256(i + 1) * 1e18,
  1385	                maxGoldIn: 0
  1386	            });
  1387	        }
  1388	        vm.prank(elder);
  1389	        OrderResult[] memory results = world.submitClanOrders(clanId, orders);
  1390	        for (uint256 i = 0; i < results.length; i++) {
  1391	            assertEq(uint8(results[i].status), uint8(StatusCode.OK), "sell order ok");
  1392	        }
  1393	
  1394	        Mission memory m = world.getActiveMission(orders[0].clansmanId);
  1395	        uint64 executeAtTick = m.settlesAtTick;
  1396	        bytes32 executedSig =
  1397	            keccak256("ScheduledMarketActionExecuted(uint32,uint32,uint8,uint8,uint256,uint8,uint256,uint64)");
  1398	
  1399	        while (world.getWorldState().currentTick < executeAtTick) {
  1400	            _advanceTick();
  1401	        }
  1402	
  1403	        vm.recordLogs();
  1404	        _advanceTick();
  1405	        Vm.Log[] memory logs = vm.getRecordedLogs();
  1406	
  1407	        uint256 executedCount;
  1408	        for (uint256 i = 0; i < logs.length; i++) {
  1409	            if (logs[i].emitter != address(world) || logs[i].topics.length == 0 || logs[i].topics[0] != executedSig) {
  1410	                continue;
  1411	            }
  1412	            (,, uint8 resourceIn, uint256 amountIn,,, uint64 settledAtTick) =
  1413	                abi.decode(logs[i].data, (uint32, ActionType, uint8, uint256, uint8, uint256, uint64));
  1414	            if (settledAtTick != executeAtTick) continue;
  1415	
  1416	            assertEq(uint32(uint256(logs[i].topics[1])), clanId, "same clan should execute");
  1417	            assertEq(resourceIn, uint8(ResourceType.Wood), "sell should input wood");
  1418	            assertEq(amountIn, uint256(executedCount + 1) * 1e18, "execution should be FIFO");
  1419	            executedCount++;
  1420	        }
  1421	
  1422	        assertEq(executedCount, 3, "three same-tick sells should execute");
  1423	        assertGt(world.getClan(clanId).goldBalance, 3e18, "clan should gain gold from sells");
  1424	    }
  1425	
  1426	    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
  1427	    // Test 16: twoClan_sellBuyCycle — clan A sells wood, clan B buys wood; both succeed
  1428	    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Phase Super-Swarm Review (R2) — PR #198 (head fdcc04b)

## SUMMARY
Core fix targets mostly look landed: market execution now uses carry instead of vault, cooldown no longer appears bypassable through the scheduled path, the sort no longer does storage writes inside the inner loop, and the CEI ordering around pool calls looks materially better. I found 2 HIGH regressions and 2 MEDIUM follow-ups: the carry rewrite strands vault inventory because there is still no `vault -> carry` path, sell-side submit validation is still incomplete, and the scheduled-queue visibility contract has been broken for downstream consumers.

## HIGH severity findings
- [ClanWorld.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:668), [ClanWorld.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1858), [ClanWorld.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2184) — The carry-based rewrite removed vault-backed selling without adding any production `vault -> carry` path. `DepositResources` is only `carry -> vault`, `MarketSell` now always debits `carry`, and `transferVaultResource()` still reverts. In practice that means starter/deposited resources are trapped in the vault and cannot be sold, and the intended wheelbarrow anti-bandit mechanic is not actually implementable.
- [ClanWorld.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1210), [ClanWorld.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1225), [ClanWorld.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1706), [ClanWorld.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2077), [ClanWorld.t.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:2533) — Sell-side submit validation is still broken. There is no upfront carry-balance check for `MarketSell`, and an immediate sell that fails for empty carry still comes back as `OrderResult.status = OK` / `marketMode = Immediate`. So callers are told the order succeeded even though no trade happened and cooldown was consumed.

## MEDIUM severity findings
- [ClanWorld.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:469), [ClanWorld.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1321), [ClanWorld.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:2510), [clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md](</home/claude/code/clan-world/docs/planning/V1/01 Blockchain Game Spec/clanworld_v4_2_state_schema_interface_spec.md:763>), [clanworld_convex_backend_spec.md](</home/claude/code/clan-world/docs/planning/V1/02 Frontend Spec/clanworld_convex_backend_spec.md:352>) — Scheduled actions are no longer created when the travel mission is accepted; they are only enqueued at settlement and then deleted in the same heartbeat. That preserves same-tick execution, but it breaks the documented queue lifecycle and makes `getScheduledMarketActionsForTick()` / `getMarketState().nextTickQueue` unable to preview pending trades.
- [ClanWorld.t.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:2533), [ClanWorld.t.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:1365), [HeartbeatOrdering.t.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/test/HeartbeatOrdering.t.sol:195) — Coverage still misses the risky edges this rewrite introduced. The new empty-carry sell test is not a reliable guard for the carry-vs-vault rule, the FIFO test only covers one clan / one batch where append order already matches commit order, and the heartbeat ordering test no longer proves the wheelbarrow-vs-same-tick-world-event interaction because the seller is given its own carry.

## LOW severity findings
- [ClanWorld.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1175), [ClanWorld.t.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:812) — The inline cooldown comment is stale. It still says market orders may fall back to the scheduled path while on cooldown, but the code and tests now reject them outright.

## Cross-cutting observations
- [ClanWorld.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1717), [ClanWorld.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1994) — The CEI fix itself looks good: sell-side carry is deducted before the external call, failed immediate sells restore carry, and buy-side balance changes happen only after the pool call succeeds.
- [IClanWorld.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:157), [IClanWorld.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:382), [IClanWorld.sol](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/src/IClanWorld.sol:541), [packages/contracts/AGENTS.md](/home/claude/code/clan-world/packages/contracts/AGENTS.md:3) — This round also changes enum ordinals, `OrderResult` layout, and several event signatures on the repo’s canonical seam. That may be intentional, but it is an integration hazard unless every consumer regenerates from the new ABI in lockstep.
- `forge test` was not runnable in this session because the workspace is read-only, so this review is from static inspection plus the existing test intent.

I found 2 HIGH regressions and 2 MEDIUM follow-ups: the carry rewrite strands vault inventory because there is still no `vault -> carry` path, sell-side submit validation is still incomplete, and the scheduled-queue visibility contract has been broken for downstream consumers.
